item.title: Ronald McDonald House Sporting Clays Tournament
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us and celebrate the changing of the seasons as we host shooters of all skill levels paired with the best shooters in the world for THE premiere sporting clays event in Central Ohio, the Ronald McDonald House Charities Sporting Clays Tournament. This year’s event will once again be held at Mad River Sportsman’s Club for a fun day of sporting clays, all to benefit families of seriously ill children. Each team will be accompanied by a pro shooter to encourage safety and to improve participants’ shooting skills. Each participant will receive 100 clay targets, ammunition, lunch and a special gift from Kids and Clays!

item.title: Sip And Paint with Dee W Ieye!
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
What Makes A Sip And Paint More Fun? A Drag Queen Of Course!
Meet us at The Olive Tree with our friends at the Crooked Door Studio!
All supplies is provided and our full bar menu, special wine menu, and small plates will be available! Feel free to come early and stay after for more drinks and fun!
The Olive Tree favorite, Dee W Ieye will be right there with you painting away, sparking up laughs, and making sure you're having a good time!
Grab a friend, a date, or perfect for a girls day out!
Cost is $35
See you there!

item.title: Jalle Berry Toxic Brew Co. at the Syndicate
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
It's the most wonderful time of the year! What started as Fitz's homebrew has now become an annual tradition as we collaborate with our pals at Toxic Brew Co. in Dayton on this raspberry jalapeño pale ale.
We'll be tapping our original recipe Jalle’, as well as a couple of variants. The Blacker The Berry The Sweeter The Juice Jalle' Berry, and Smokin' Hot Jalle' Berry at 5:00PM sharp. Be sure to grab a pint and/or growlers of all three!! Our good pal Jeff Anderson of Pretty Good Posters hooked us up with some rad new can art! Four packs and shirts featuring the new design will be available for purchase!

item.title: Stained Glass Workshop
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Join us for a magical afternoon of creativity and connection at our Triple Moon Stained Glass Workshop!
Our expert instructor will guide you through the art of stained glass! This is a beginner friendly workshop explaining the steps and techniques and helping you create your very own Triple Moon Stained Glass piece. All materials will be provided, so just bring yourself and your enthusiasm!
This workshop is not just about learning a new skill—it’s about connecting with others in our community who share your interest. Discover the joy of working with stained glass and leave with a beautiful piece to adorn your home or give as a unique gift.
The Details:
Mark your calendars for Saturday, June 22nd from 1:00PM to 4:00 PM. Our guest instructor for this event is Stacey from Windyknolls Stained Glass. All materials will be provided.
Tickets:
- $65 per guest and you get to take home your finished project.
- No refunds for no shows. In the event you must cancel your reservation, if it’s 72-hour notice, you may request a refund via Windyknolls Stained Glass.
- If someone in your party does not come, you may not take home their materials.
- No-shows and cancellations made the same day as class will not be refunded.
- You must be registered for the event to take the workshop; no same day registrations or walk-ins.
- No children under the age of 12 please.
The Venue:
The **New** Triple Moon Creative Lounge
117 West Columbus Ave. Bellefontaine, OH 43311. Free parking is available on West

The Jesse James Gang Live in Concert

item.title: 2024 Crawfish Boil featuring Bent Water Brewing Co.
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
We are excited to partner with Bent Water Brewing for our 2024 Crawfish Boil on Tuesday, June 25th from 6-10 PM!
Bent Water Brewing was born from the mind of Bellefontaine native, Aaron Reams who is a scientist, artist, and beer enthusiast. From there, the Bent Water team formed, drawing in unique expertise to strike a balance between the artistic and scientific complexities of brewing and operating a brewery. The result? Ingenuity & Predictability – Creativity & Quality – Variety & Familiarity.
Bent Water Brewing is proud to call Lynn, MA home because of the city’s rich industrial roots and superior water source. Bent Water believes passion can turn industry into art; and with careful, creative manipulation, they bend water into unique, flavorful beer.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: The Syndicate - 213 S Main Street, Bellefontaine OH
Crawfish Boil featuring Bent Water Brewing Co

item.title: Beginner's Sewing Workshop at Triple Moon
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Have you ever wanted to learn how to sew? Join us for a fun and informative sewing workshop where you’ll craft your very own customizable zipper pouch from scratch! No experience necessary – we’ll provide the sewing machines and all the fabric and materials you need. You will have several precut fabric options to customize your project with!

During this workshop, you’ll:
• Learn how to operate a sewing machine and master the basics
• Receive step-by-step instructions from an experienced seamstress
• Create and customize your own pouch complete with liner and zipper.

We’re excited to host this event at Triple Moon Creative Lounge, a space designed to foster creativity and community. Don’t miss this chance to learn a new skill and create something beautiful! Stop in early to make a candle at our candle bar!

The Details:
This event is Sunday, September 8th, from 12:00 to 4:00 PM. You get to choose your fabric the from several precut fabric options when you arrive! All materials and sewing machines will be provided.

Tickets:
- $65 per guest and you get to take home your finished project.
- No refunds for no shows. In the event you must cancel your reservation, if it’s 72-hour notice, you may request a refund via the refund option on Eventbrite or email triplemoonproducts@gmail.com and be refunded.
- If you cannot make it to your class and you are outside of the refund window, you may not transfer your ticket to another date or be refunded.
- Refunds will not be handled on a case by case basis, we cannot make exceptions regardless of the circumstances. (We apologize but this is our livelihood, so if it’s past the refund window we cannot refund you. Materials for class are already bought and prepped, so we are sorry for any inconvenience!)
- If someone in your party does not come, you may not take home their materials.
- No-shows and cancellations made the same day as class will not be refunded.
- You must be registered for the event to take the workshop; no same day registrations or walk-ins.
- No children under the age of 13 please.

The Venue:
The **New** Triple Moon Creative Lounge
117 West Columbus Ave. Bellefontaine, OH 43311. Free parking is available on West Columbus, there is a huge parking lot in the back of the building with free parking as well!

The City:
Downtown Bellefontaine is a beautiful place to spend the day! If you are coming in from out of town please plan extra time to visit all the best places! Tons of coffee, shopping, antiques, bakeries, and FOOD! We are also in D.O.R.A., so grab a drink and walk around! Feel free to email or dm for any local recommendations!

item.title: Sicilian Wine Dinner
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Join us for our March Wine Club as we feature Food + Wines from Sicily! Our very own Bethany Kingrey & Reilly Robson and Executive Chef Jason Plank will be on-site describing each of the four dishes and 2-ounce wine pairings. Bottles will be available for sale during the event and at the end of the evening. The Syndicate will be following social distancing guidelines to ensure the safety of our guests. We encourage any larger parties wanting to attend to purchase your tickets as a group – this will guarantee you and your party will seat together and enjoy your dinner side by side. MENU Course 1 Arancini Carbonara: Pancetta, Pecorino, Pistachio Pesto Pairing: 2014 Cottanera Etna Rosso “Contrada Feudo Di Mezzo" Course 2 Sicilian Orange Salad: Blood Oranges, Fennel, Onion, Mint, Caviar Pairing: 2019 Conttanera Bianco “Barbazzale" Course 3 Veal Osso Bucco: Center Cut Braised Veal Shank, Risotto Milanese, Gremolata Pairing: 2017 Occhipinti Siccagno Nero d Avola Course 4 Cannoli: Ruby Chocolate, Ricotta Cream Pairing: 2017 Conttanera Etna Rosso “Contrada Diciasettesalme” Tickets: $67
We are excited to see you soon! Cheers!

item.title: St. Patrick's Day Menu Takeover at Brewfontaine
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us for one of our most popular menu swaps of the year! Full food and drink menu coming soon! Starters: Shamrock Pretzels: Shamrock shaped pretzel topped with green salt and served with a side of Irish Stout beer cheese $7 Warm German Potato Salad: Sliced potatoes, red bell peppers, white onion, and bacon marinated in sweet oil and vinegar, topped with parsley and bacon. Served hot and ready! $6 Soups: Creamy Reuben Soup: White cream and swiss cheese soup filled with onion, celery, sauerkraut, and corned beef, topped with rye croutons $5.95 Irish Bacon and Cabbage Soup: Potatoes, bacon, and cabbage swimming in a tomato/beef stock topped with green onions $5.95 Entrees: Cabbage Roll: Ground beef and white rice stuffed and rolled into a cabbage leaf, topped with sweet red sauce $13 Irish Grilled Cheese Panini: Horseradish cheddar, sharp white cheddar, tomatoes, bacon, and Dijon mustard $9.50 Bangers and Mashed: Sausage and sauerkraut served over colcannon Irish mashed potatoes with Dijon cream sauce $12 Irish Egg Salad Sandwich: Hard boiled eggs, mayonnaise, diced tomatoes, butter lettuce, green onions, and house seasoning tossed together making a creamy salad served on toasted Italian artisan bread $8.50 Grilled Reuben: Corned Beef and sauerkraut on toasted rye topped with melted Swiss and thousand island dressing $9.50 Dessert: Shamrock Cheese Cake: Mint chocolate cookie crust, green mint cheesecake, topped with chocolate ganache, whipped topping, and gold sprinkles $8.50 Beers: Bircus Kilpatrick's One Legged Dry Stout · 4.5% · Ludlow, KY Straight-forward, bold & traditional Dry Irish Stout recipe. R Shea Irish Red Light Irish Red Ale · 6.2% · Akron, OH We didn't sacrifice the alcohol in making this beer. We kept this Irish Red at 6.2% ABV but lightened up the body for easy drinkability. Made with Akron-Grown Hydroponic Hops and Westbranch Malts from Brunswick, Ohio. Restoration Stewarts Irish Red Irish Red Ale · 5.9% · Delaware, OH Smooth drinking Irish red. Malty with notes of bread crust, toffee and light roast. Finish is dry and balanced, with enough bitterness to balance the malt. Clean. Dalton Union Smoothie Hard Cider Sweet Cider 5.5% Marysville, OH A yummy combination of strawberry, kiwi and banana...Tinted green for St. Paddy’s Day! Warped Wing 10 Ton Irish Cream Stout 7% Dayton, OH A special spin on our beloved Oatmeal Stout with rich hints of coffee, chocolate, vanilla and a roasted finish. Roll up your sleeves and dig in. Outerbelt Cloverleaf Dry Stout 4.5% Carroll, OH Notes of smooth coffee and approachable bitter chocolate, with British malted barley Cheers!

item.title: Easter Egg-Stravaganza
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us as we celebrate the return of spring with a super fun Easter-themed event! Hunt for Easter eggs scattered throughout downtown from 5-8pm and complete our scavenger hunt along the way! Pick up your scavenger hunt from from any area downtown business and get busy hopping around identifying all of the Easter surprises in our storefronts! Turn in your passport next to the Courthouse fountain on Main Street and receive a goody bag! One lucky kid will even win a $100 Fun Company gift card in our random drawing. Not enough fun for you? Susie at The Photo Booth will have Easter set ups in her studio and photos are available for just $20. We hear the Easter Bunny may even make an appearance! Don't forget to sip and shop your way around all weekend long... many stores have late hours and specials, and it's a DORA weekend - meaning from 4-10pm on Friday and noon-10pm on Saturday you can carry around your beer or wine purchased at any downtown bar or restaurant! This entire event is free and we can't wait to see you in Downtown Bellefontaine!

item.title: Natal Nourishment-A Prenatal Yoga Series with Mac
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Public · Anyone on or off Facebook Natal Nourishment, the first of the prenatal and postnatal/postpartum yoga series' to be offered by Certified Yoga Teacher and Certified Birth Doula, MacKenzie Myers Fitzpatrick in 2021. This 5-week series offers pregnant individuals a journey of going inward for the purpose of observing the body, moving with intention, and gaining knowledge on the ways in which the body changes during pregnancy and birth. No yoga experience needed. Over the course of the 5 weeks we will uncover topics such as: -Expanding the Breath/Meditation practice (deepening the breath, intention setting, tuning inward) -Muscular Movement in Whole Body with Concentrations on the Pelvic Floor (postures to promote contraction/relaxation, home practice resources) -Comfort Measures Pregnancy/Labor (massage, self, support of others, props, environment) -Nourishment (consumption, sleep, stimuli, growing baby) -Anatomical & Physiological Understandings (shifts in mobility & comfort, hormonal changes, safety in the body) Within each intimate hour and a half class, students will develop a rhythm of integrating the practices of breath, movement, comfort, and discussion in a calming environment at Homegrown Yoga. Each week diving a bit deeper in the rhythms while we dedicate time to discussing a new highlighted topic. Mac provides an environment in which discussion of any issues, discomforts, concerns are honored, respected, and valued. Prioritizing the value of the inner-wisdom that each participant brings to this collective experience surrounding pregnancy and birth. At the end of this 5 week series you will leave with a tool kit of experiential and informational resources to support you in your journey through pregnancy and transitions into parenting. Wherever you are at in your pregnancy, you are welcome to join. Variations will be made to postures depending on gestational age, yoga experience, and physical ability. This series will be offered in a sliding scale format, asking that you are mindful of your income, budget and the value of this experience in your journey. Options are as follows: A) $65 B) $75 C) $85 Book now through www.homegrownyoga.fit or by calling (937) 404-1461. Sunday's from 1-2:30 pm Week 1 Feb 21 Week 2 Feb 28 Week 3 Mar 7 Week 4 Mar 14 Week 5 Mar 21 Due to the COVID-19 Pandemic, we are upholding our safety measures and limited the capacity of this class to 6 participants. Additionally, props at the studio are cleaned/laundered regularly. Homegrown Yoga also ensures air quality by running a HEPA filter during its hours of operation providing every participant with a healthy and safe experience. Things to Bring/Wear: -Mask (must be worn when up & moving) -Yoga Mat -Water Bottle -Throw Blanket/Mexican Blanket from home (optional) -Comfortable Clothes (that are easy to move in, comfortable for your body, & accommodating to any temperature changes your body may experience.) We look forward to working with you on this journey!

item.title: Exploring Meditation at Homegrown Yoga
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Meditation has many benefits. These benefits have been documented through the centuries by yogis, monks, seekers, and more recently by scientists. Benefits of meditation can include reduced stress, better sleep, healthy changes in brain structure, inner peace, and spiritual awakening. Over this six-week course, we will explore some of the main tenets of mindfulness-based meditation. By experimenting with various meditation practices, this course will help you develop a home practice that suits your own style and schedule. This course can also provide a deeper exploration for experienced meditators. During the 6 weeks we will cultivate a community of support for our practice and allow ample time and space for questions and feedback during each class as well as instructor support between classes. We will touch on meditation set-up and mindfulness-based techniques including Concentration, Heart-Centered, and Subtle Body practices. Requirements for this course include a commitment to sitting in meditation 10-15 minutes per day on your own between classes. There will be optional texts that accompany the class. Your investment is $60. Book online at homegrownyoga.fit

item.title: First Fridays Art and Wine Walk
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Get an early start to your weekend at Downtown Bellefontaine’s Annual Art & Wine Walk. Stroll the streets of revitalized Downtown Bellefontaine while you sip and shop, enjoying wine, art and music. Event sponsored by Six Hundred Downtown.
Check-in The evening begins at 4 p.m. with check-in at The Marketplace, 130 S Main St. (back entrance). Once checked in, attendees receive an event map and 10 tasting tickets. You will then embark on a tasting tour of Downtown Bellefontaine. Disposable cups will be provided at each stop. VIP ticketholders will also receive a commemorative wine-tasting glass and a ticket for the Holland Theatre's comedy show at check in.
Entertainment As wine walkers stroll from shop to shop, they can enjoy live music and artists at participating businesses. Savor your tasting while taking in the beauty of the artwork on display.
Wine Sampling Each location will be serving a different kind of wine sourced from local providers. The walk is filled with a variety of red and white wines with some specialty selections. While enjoying wine samples at each participating location.
VIP Perks VIP ticket holders will receive a commemorative glass and a ticket to the Roots Comedy Ramble with Mike Larsen hosted by The Holland Theatre. Show doors open at 7:30. More show details can be found at: https://www.facebook.com/events/751220939089884/
Ticket Sales and Pricing Tickets are $45 for VIP and $25 for Regular admission. Tickets available via Eventbrite. All sales are final.

item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Come Celebrate With Us! Local Artists! Live Music! Food Truck! 8am-8pm Meet local artists, watch live paintings, and shop art vendors outside on our patio! Brought to you by our good friends at The Cracked Pot Studio! 9am-12:30pm Hang out with The Band Hannah as they entertain and sing popular covers you will know out on our patio! 5pm-8pm Come back and enjoy an exclusive experience with The Band Hannah featuring their original music! Come and go as you please, $5 at the door! First come first served seating! 5pm-8pm Check out the NEW mouthwatering food truck Biscuit Boss!!!! Their menu is amazing!!! Thank you for ALL the support and helping us get through this first and CRAZY year! We can’t wait to see you!

item.title: First Fridays Spring Fling
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: POSTPONED* NEW DATE 6/11/21 Join us in Downtown Bellefontaine on May 11th for an event of epic proportions; The Spring Fling! Thanks to a generous donation from Honda Federal Credit Union, kids young and old can enjoy 21 different carnival games, a zipline down Main Street, and an inflatable axe throwing contest! Fair-style food trucks will also be setup during the event. Inflatable axe throwing competition will be a bracket style event hosted by Roundhouse Depot. his is an individual competition and there are only 20 spots available. Tickets are first come first serve and must be purchased online prior to the event (see details below). First and second place will win a merch basket from Roundhouse Depot. The event will run from 5-8pm. Tickets are available via Eventbrite for $25. Your ticket gets you unlimited plays at all carnival games as well as rides on the zip line. Tickets will also available be available onsite the day of event for $30. Axe throwing competition tickets are available via Eventbrite ($20) and must be purchased by May 3rd to participate. All ticket sales are final

item.title: Outdoor Sunset Reiki and Restore
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Join Addy and Heather for an evening of relaxation and rejuvenation. This class combines restorative yoga with reiki. Addy will guide you through your practice while Heather will offer reiki adjustments throughout the class. This class will take place outside at Addy's house around a scenic pond (as seen in picture). Following class, feel free to stay and enjoy a beautiful sunset. Reiki is a Japanese technique for pain and stress relief that promotes healing, relaxation, feelings of peace, and a restful nights sleep. The word (Rei) means “Higher power/ Wisdom” and (Ki) means “life force energy”.
It is a safe and natural method use to create balance within. The Practitioner uses the gentle placement of hands (about the weight of a nickel) on various areas of the fully-clothed body, creating space for your inner healer to do it’s work. Personal Investment: $20 Book through the mindbody app or www.homegrownyoga.fit
Prior to class, you will be emailed the address for the class location. Rain date: May 23rd. Homegrown Yoga Downtown, 115 N Main Street, Bellefontaine, United State

item.title: A Taste of Cork + Board Wine Event
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
RSVP your free ticket for “A Taste of Cork + Board” Wine Event at BUILD Cowork + Space in Bellefontaine, OH June 18th, 4:30-7:30pm!
A charcuterie spread, including unique cheeses, local hand-crafted salami, and mouth-watering condiments such as gourmet olives, pickles, peppers, jams, breads, and chocolates. The charcuterie boards will be complimented by samples of the low-intervention wines offered at the Cork + Board location in Downtown Springfield, OH. Full-size retail items will also be available for sale at the event.
Join us for a “Happy” hour of discovering the foods, savoring the flavors, experiencing the fun…and wine!

item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Downtown businesses will be decked out in Disney/Pixar displays and will be hosting Trick-or-Treat
No costume yet? No problem! We will have face painters on site all evening.
There will be a ton of family friendly activities all night. Putt & Play will be hosting a FREE haunted house and $5 glow putt for kids. The Photo Booth will be hosting spooky photoshoots at their studio. Food trucks will be on site. Candy-cornhole, sack races, mummy wrapping, and as well as additional games will be setup on site.

item.title: Bellefontaine Culinary Week
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us for Bellefontaine’s first ever Culinary Week! This event is family friendly and free to participate. Pick up your dining passport at the Logan County Chamber of Commerce (100 S Main Street) starting Friday, September 3rd. Your passport will have three stamp spaces. Visit any participating location and make a qualifying purchase to redeem a stamp in their designated spot. Once you’ve collected three stamps fill out your contact info on the reverse side and return your passport back to the Chamber. The event will end Sunday, September 12th. We will then pull passports raffle style for gift cards to all participating locations on Monday, the 13th. DORA will also be extended to run from September 3rd-12th.

item.title: Holiday Open House
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
It’s almost time to kick off holiday season in Downtown Bellefontaine!
Visit our wonderful downtown stores for holiday displays, special promotions, and products you won't find anywhere else
DORA will be in effect Friday, Saturday, and Sunday. It’s the perfect time to grab a drink and get your holiday shopping done!
More information to come - "like" and share this event to stay in the loop as our plans and promotions are announced

item.title: Still Standing: A tribute to Elton John
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Musicians perform the music of Elton John........STILL STANDING are Elton John. Formed in 2013 STILL STANDING are the modern visual and musical Elton John Event bringing all the timeless classics to your venue with the attention to detail that his music deserves. With over 200 Million Albums sold worldwide, Elton John along with the lyrical tapestry of Bernie Taupin have been a soundtrack to our lives. His catalog is comparable to the top selling artist of all time. STILL STANDING are the band that strive to carry on their legacy. Fronted by Ben "Almost Elton" Frey who has been referred to as the closest representation of Elton's vocal on the Tribute Circuit, STILL STANDING will provide your venue with THE up close Elton John Tribute Experience!

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Still Standing: A Tribute to Elton John

item.title: Napoleon Dynamite Screening and Q&A
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
This unique evening includes a full screening of the beloved indie classic Napoleon Dynamite, followed by a lively, freewheeling, moderated discussion with fan-favorite cast members Jon Heder (Napoleon Dynamite), and Efren Ramirez (Pedro).
Appealing to the inner teenager in each of us, Napoleon Dynamite's dialogue makes it one of the most quoted movies of our time. Much has changed since the film's release 15 years ago, but the characters -- as enduring as they are endearing -- stay in our hearts.

item.title: West Central Ohio Community Concert Band at Holland Theatre
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join the West Central Ohio Community Concert Band for "Holland for the Holidays," an evening of holiday favorites presented at no charge for the community. The WCOC Concert Band is celebrating its 29th season in 2021! Directed by Mr. Myles Bowers, the all-volunteer band is organized as a nonprofit. Members include amateur musicians, professional musicians, educators, and students hailing from multiple Ohio counties. Over the years the band has performed in Bellefontaine, Urbana, Marysville, Marion, Indian Lake, The Ohio State Fair, and even Washington, D.C. This year, the band looks forward to closing its season by bringing the sounds of holiday cheer to downtown Bellefontaine. Seating will be on a first-come, first-served basis.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: WCOC Concert Band at Holland Theatre

item.title: Squirrel Nut Zippers Holiday Caravan Tour at Holland Theatre
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
With their quirky mix of jazz chords, folk music, and punk rock leanings, the Squirrel Nut Zippers will perform a mix of classic holiday favorites and SNZ hits at this special Holiday Caravan show.
“Jimbo Mathus and his merry nine-piece ensemble create music that exists outside of time and space in a domain that includes Jazz, swing and R & B, not to mention klezmer and Balkan music,” noted one review of the band's most recent album, The Lost Songs of Doc Souchon. “It all gets thrown into the pot to see what develops, and what emerges might sound like ‘50s rock blended with juke jazz sitting next to a banjo tune. That sense of never knowing what comes next is part of what makes the Zippers so intriguing."

item.title: Wine Club - Napa Valley
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Four course wine pairing and dinner from Napa Valley 1st Course BonAnno, Chardonnay Carneros (2019) Tasting Notes: BonAnno is good year in Italian. White flowers, apple blossom, fresh peach, ripe lemon, crisp green apple, white peaches, hints of vanilla and brioche. Garlic and Chive stuffed baked Brie served with apple compote, honey, and nuts. 2nd Course Chateau Montelena Winery, Zinfandel Calistoga (2017) Tasting Notes: Cinnamon, all spice, cherry, torrent of red fruit, tobacco, dark and lush, tart raspberry, blackberry jam, red licorice, earthiness, with black pepper and star anise. Lobster and Chorizo stuffed Poblano, with boursin, avocado crème, fried corn tortilla strips. 3nd Course Logan Farrell, Cabernet Sauvignon Napa Valley (2019) Tasting Notes: dense yet energetic, showing sense of polish. Dark plum, blackberry, fig, subtle roasted applewood, caramel, hints of bakers Chocolate, toasted Oak finish. Sous vide Airline Chicken Breast, pan seared then served on a bed of goat cheese, potato puree, finished with jus, crispy prosciutto, and wilted arugula. 4th Course Abraxas, Vin de Terroir Scintilla Sonoma Vineyard Carneros (2017) Tasting Notes: Spiked Stone fruit, lime, mineral, yeasty aromas, orange, passionfruit, peach, white pineapple, green apple, twist meyer lemon, chamomile, buttercup, honeysuckle, soft herbs, lead to a floral bouquet. Key Lime Pie Sweet Rolls, with graham cracker, pistachio, and lime cream cheese icing. For future wine club events, please visit us at syndicatedowntown.com/events

item.title: Bourbon Club - Watershed
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join The Syndicate for our bourbon tasting and food pairing with Watershed as our bourbon. Limited seats are available and the event is 21+. There are 2 tickets types, general admission and VIP. General admission will get you in for the bourbon and food pairing. Our VIP experience is limited to 10 guests. The VIP experience ticket gets you into our bourbon and food pairing, but in addition you will get to sit at our bar with our bourbon steward to ask questions and to see how one of cocktails is crafted. This experience starts prior to our tasting and will start at 5:15 and ending at 6:00pm. To purchase tickets, visit: https://syndicatedowntown.com/events/

item.title: Heavy Metal Honky Tonk Duo LIVE
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Half of your favorite Heavy Metal Honky Tonk duo is coming to visit and bringing our good pal James Hunnicutt with him! Come Join us for some late night jams!! For other live music, please check out brewfontaine.com

item.title: The BARbell Crawl, Presented by Anytime Fitness
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: With the 2022 upon us, we often start looking towards our New Year’s resolutions. Oftentimes exercise programs that we start in the New Year are abandoned by Easter. January’s First Friday is designed to help you find a movement program that you actually enjoy! The more you love to do it, the more likely you are to stick with it. Join us in downtown Bellefontaine for our first annual BARbell Crawl! Here’s how it works... With the purchase of your ticket, you’ll get to sample 5 different classes at each of the exercise based businesses in downtown Bellefontaine: Homegrown Yoga, LifeReps, Barbelle Yoga and Fitness, The Loco Depot, and Anytime Fitness. After all your classes are completed, join us at Roundhouse for an after party! Each fitness studio will be there to answer any questions you might have. Memberships to each facility will be raffled off following the crawl. Come hang out, drink a beer, and get to know new people because life is all about finding balance. Tickets: $35 (includes all 5 sample classes, event t-shirt, raffle ticket, and one beer at Roundhouse) Check-in: 5:30-5:50 pm in the Marketplace Class 1: 6:00-6:15 pm Class 2: 6:25-6:40 pm Class 3: 6:50-7:05 pm Class 4: 7:15-7:30 pm Class 5: 7:40-7:55 pm After Party: 8 pm at Roundhouse Space is extremely limited! Purchase your tickets today!

item.title: Evening of Art, Wine & Music

item.title: Girl Named Tom
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: PLEASE NOTE: THIS SHOW IS SOLD OUT. With their distinctive harmonies and heartfelt performances, sibling trio Bekah, Caleb, and Joshua Liechty became instant favorites on Season 21 of NBC’s The Voice. Their Holland performance will feature a mix of original songs and the acoustic pop, country, and folk covers that captured the hearts – and votes -- of viewers across the country. PLEASE NOTE: Until further notice, all staff, volunteers, and audience members at the Holland Theatre are asked to wear a mask upon entry and in all common areas of the theater. Once seated, audience members may remove masks while eating and drinking. Please note that the Holland reserves the right to modify our policies in response to artists' requests, the local health situation, and the constantly shifting nature of the pandemic and its variants. Please provide your email address when purchasing tickets so that we can keep you informed of any changes.

item.title: The Chocolate Walk, Presented by City Sweets & Creamery
item.dc:creator: admin_sn

item.title: The Young Fables: The Screenplay Tour
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: The Young Fables are the country/Americana duo of Laurel Wright and Wesley Lunsford, modern proclaimers of classic country’s storytelling roots. The evening will include a live performance from the duo as well as a screening of their new film, The Fable of a Song. This award-winning film traces the process of writing one song, “Daddy’s Girl,” and its intersection with heartbreaking, unexpected family loss. Check out a trailer for The Fable of a Song here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Xq2fN6YzraM

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: <iframe title="THE FABLE OF A SONG - Feature Documentary - OFFICIAL TRAILER" width="500" height="281" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/Xq2fN6YzraM?feature=oembed" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture" allowfullscreen></iframe>

item.title: Tom Chess & Ravi Padmanabha
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Experience an evening of mystical world music informed by Asian and Middle Eastern traditions with these two multi-instrumentalists and composers. Best known for his jazz-inspired work with the oud and ney, Chess is joined by percussionist Padmanabha; together, their improvisation mesmerizes and delights.

item.title: Ekstasis Duo
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Classical duo Eliran Avny and Natasha Farny present “… From the Shadows,” a vibrant, joyous, and deeply moving program featuring under-represented composers including George Walker and Alma Mahler.
Juilliard graduates, pianist Avny and cellist Farny are acclaimed soloists in their own right; together they create a truly uplifting evening of classical music.

item.title: Oshima Brothers @ The Holland Theatre
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Maine-based indie duo Oshima Brothers have been creating music together since childhood. The brothers blend songs from the heart with lush harmonies to produce what NPR described as a "roots-based pop sound that is infectious." On stage, Sean and Jamie create a full and complex sound with dynamic vocals, electric and acoustic guitars, octave bass, loops, and percussion.
This show is part of the historic Holland Theatre's Backstage series, an intimate concert experience with limited seating. Performers and audience members will all be on the stage.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: The Holland Theatre, 127 E. Columbus, Bellefontaine

item.title: Valentine's Weekend at the Syndicate
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Book your Valentine's reservation with us! Prime rib special for dinner on Saturday, 2/12. For other special events, visit syndicatedowntown.com

item.title: Not So Bad Art Celebration Event and Auction
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Eight Logan County celebrities have each created artwork that has been on display at The Olive Tree, 121 W Chillicothe in downtown Bellefontaine. This is the night we celebrate! Doors open at 5pm for a ticketed pre-auction event of drinks, appetizers and continued voting. At 6pm the winner of the popular vote traveling trophy will be announced. An auction of all the art will follow. The auction is free to attend. All proceeds benefit the Logan County Art League.

item.title: Romantic Valentine Banners at Craft Paper Scissors
item.dc:creator: admin_sn

item.title: February Paint n Sip
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Marilyn has been doing several barn quilts lately, inspiring this quilt square. It is done on a 16x16 outdoor-ready wood, sealed for the weather.

item.title: Bourbon Club - Buffalo Trace Rare Finds
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join The Syndicate for our bourbon tasting and food pairing with rare bourbon finds including ﻿﻿Blantons, EH Taylor, Eagle Rare, and Buffalo Trace. Limited seats are available and the event is 21+. There are 2 tickets types, general admission and VIP. General admission will get you in for the bourbon and food pairing. Our VIP experience is limited to 10 guests. The VIP experience ticket gets you into our bourbon and food pairing, but in addition you will get to sit at our bar with our bourbon steward to ask questions and to see how one of cocktails is crafted. This experience starts prior to our tasting and will start at 5:15 and ending at 6:00pm. If you would like to be seated with someone specifically, please put it in the ticket notes. For future events, please visit syndicatedowntown.com

item.title: Brewfontaine 4th Annual Girl Scout Cookie & Beer Pairing
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us for our 4th Annual Girl Scout cookie & beer pairing. One of our most popular events every year! Due to increased interest, this event will be held next door at The Syndicate. Each ticket includes five different 5oz beers paired with five different Girl Scout cookie varieties (2 cookies per variety). Including a never before released cookie! Feel free to stop into Brewfontaine prior to grab dinner before coming over to satisfy your sweet tooth! Additional boxes of cookies can be purchased at the event as well! If you would like to be seated with someone or a group in particular, please be sure to answer the seating questions when purchasing your ticket at the link below. Pairing #1 Tagalongs - Crispy, vanilla cookies layered with peanut butter, covered with chocolate - Roundhouse Depot Peanut butter Blonde Blonde Ale 5.3% Our newest peanut butter beer is a light refreshing blonde flavored with peanut butter powder and a few chocolate nibs. Pairing #2 Girl Scout S’Mores - Graham sandwich cookies with chocolate and marshmallowy filling - Coronado Bonfire Beach Pastry Stout 10.5% Stout with Vanilla, Cinnamon, Chocolate, Honey, Brown Sugar & Lactose. Is the best part of a beach bonfire the s'mores or the beers? We think the answer is both. Bonfire Beach blends all the flavors you love from freshly toasted s'mores into a rich, decadent, pastry stout. Needless to say, this beer is fire. Pairing #3 Samoas - Crisp cookies layered with peanut butter and covered with a chocolaty coating - Weidimann’s German Chocolate Cake Imperial Milk Stout 10.6% This Stout is your favorite dessert in a glass. With wonderful aromas of chocolate and toasted coconut along with flavors of rich chocolate toasted coconut and subtle notes of caramel and vanilla. This full bodied stout is sure to warm you up with its 10.6% ABV. Pairing #4 Adventurfuls (NEW!!) - Indulgent brownie-inspired cookies with caramel flavored crème and a hint of sea salt - North High Coffee Crop Blonde Ale 4.5% Local beer made with local ingredients, infused with locally roasted Thunderkiss coffee. Pairing #5 Trefoils - Traditional shortbread cookies - Ciderboy’s Strawberry Magic Cider 5% Come alive to crisp apple hard cider blended with the fresh taste of strawberries, soft and deep red, backed by mouth watering sweetness and a delicate hint of tartness. This is Strawberry Magic Hard Cider kicked up with a hint of Saturday night. We blend it a little differently around here. If you’ve never tried it, get ready for confetti. This event is age 21+ and tickets are non-refundable. For additional events, visit brewfontaine.com

item.title: RDBC Co. X Rise Bakehouse Pairing
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join RDBC and Rise Bakehouse for a pairing of 4 of Roundhouse’s tasty brews and 4 fantastic creation from Rise. A match made in carb heaven!

item.title: Charcuterie Workshop
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Create Your Very Own Charcuterie Board! Create a charcuterie board with Tyler and learn a few tricks when it comes to placing and designing your plate. We will also go over how to make the very popular and eye catching salami rosette! BYOB is welcomed so grab a group of friends and your favorite bottle of wine! Perfect for girls night out! We will be using a 12in round palm leaf plate to make our boards. You may eat as we go or take it home for a special occasion. All materials will be included and feature local Blue Jacket Dairy cheeses and high quality meats. This is perfect for Valentines Day!

item.title: Taste of Bellefontaine, presented by Fathom Realty
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Mark your calendar for the most delicious day of the year in Downtown Bellefontaine! The Taste of Bellefontaine, presented by Fathom Realty (Malia Hughes & Lori Newman) is scheduled for March 4th from 5-8pm. A very special tasting menu has been prepared for you to sample as you stroll your way through downtown Bellefontaine. Your ticket gets you a passport to visit participating food and beverage stops and a $10 voucher toward your full Restaurant Week meal the following week. Complete your passport and you could win big! The First Fridays series in downtown Bellefontaine is generously sponsored by Mary Rutan Health. BELLEFONTAINE’S BEST BITE: No food event is complete without a friendly competition! Scan the QR code at each stop to rank your sample. These scores will be tallied at the end of the evening and the business with the most points will be awarded Bellefontaine’s Best Bite trophy. MISC INFORMATION: Check-in will be from 5-6pm at the Main Street Marketplace. All ticket sales are final.

item.title: Lorrie Morgan Live in Bellefontaine (With Jesse Keith Whitley)
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Catch Lorrie Morgan live in concert with special guest opener, Jesse Keith Whitley. Doors open at 5 pm, show begins at 6 pm.

item.title: Michelle Robinson w/Brian Gilliam at the Syndicate
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Live music with Michelle Robinson Band in our Beer Garden from 7-10pm. Free admission for all live music!
Enter in at the Beer Garden Entrance on the side of The Syndicate building if you are looking to attend our live music. If you are looking for regular dinner service, we would suggest booking a reservation on our website to guarantee a table inside of our restaurant. Make a reservation bookthesyndicate.com
Tables are first come, first serve in the beer garden.
If you are at a table, you will have a server for food and drinks. If you don't have a table, you can still order from food and drinks from our mobile bar.
Outside food and alcohol is not permitted into the premises. There are designated smoking areas outside of the fencing area, but smoking and vaping are not permitted inside the fenced areas.
In case of inclement weather, the event may be canceled or rescheduled.
For parking, please review our parking map at https://syndicatedowntown.com/parking-map/
Should you have any questions, please reach out to events@syndicatedowntown.com
For additional events, please visit syndicatedowntown.com/events

item.title: Paulo Padilha e Bando
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Catch the samba swing of Brazil with Paulo Padilha e Bando. Born in São Paulo, Padilha travels between the sounds of traditional samba and other Brazilian popular musical styles. His band of master samba musicians and dancers will make you want to dance all the way home!
Padilha appears as part of Arts Midwest World Fest, which connects international musicians with select midwestern communities. His visit to the region also includes an educational component. A music teacher with more than 25 years of experience, Padilha is a master musician and educator who specializes in rhythm and song workshops for children, teenagers, and adults.
This show is part of our Backstage Series, an intimate concert experience with the audience seated on the stage.

item.title: Bill and the Belles
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Vintage country with a contemporary twist, Bill and the Belles’ trademark charming sound is full of life, humor, and tongue-in-cheek explorations of love and loss. As Rolling Stone put it, this stringband quartet is “committed to helping early country music remain appreciated – not just replicated.”
Bill and the Belles is Kris Truelsen on guitar, fiddler Kalia Yeagle, bassist Andrew Small, and banjo/banjo-uke player Aidan VanSuetendael.
Check out their sound here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=g4RujkZPsFg

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: <iframe title="Bill and the Belles | Taking Back My Yesterday (NPR Tiny Desk Entry 2019)" width="500" height="281" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/g4RujkZPsFg?feature=oembed" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture" allowfullscreen></iframe>

item.title: Croce Plays Croce
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
A.J. Croce performs Croce Plays Croce, featuring a complete set of classics by his late father Jim Croce, some of his own tunes, and songs that influenced both him and his father. A.J. weaves in stories of his musical connection to his father, painting a picture for the audience of family, artistry, and memory.
PLEASE NOTE: This is the new date for a show previously postponed due to Covid. All tickets from previous dates are still valid, with all original seat assignments intact. Please contact the box office (937-592-9002; office@thehollandtheatre.net) with questions or to have misplaced tickets re-issued.

item.title: Germán López and Antonio Toledo: Canela y Limón
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
A brilliant young player of the five-stringed Spanish timple, Germán López honors the folk traditions of his native Canary Islands alongside flamenco, West African rhythm, jazz, and a 21st-century approach to performing “island music.” He appears with guitarist Antonio Toledo to present Canela y Limón.
Check out the pair's sound here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=HtqthqdBG6A

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: <iframe title="Germán López - Imaginando Folías" width="500" height="281" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/HtqthqdBG6A?feature=oembed" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture" allowfullscreen></iframe>

item.title: Lunasa with Daoirí Farrell
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
The finest traditional Irish instrumental band of recent times, Lunasa guarantees audiences a rollicking, musically stunning show. Lunasa played the Holland to great acclaim in 2017, and we are eager to welcome them back just before Saint Patrick’s Day. They appear with special guest Daoirí Farrell, the Dublin-born traditional singer and bouzouki player. Lúnasa is composed of Kevin Crawford (flutes, low whistles and tin whistles), Trevor Hutchinson (double bass), Ed Boyd (guitar), Seán Smyth (fiddle and low whistle) and Cillian Vallely (uilleann pipes and low whistles).
PLEASE NOTE: This is the new date for a show previously postponed due to Covid. All tickets from previous dates are still valid, with all original seat assignments intact. Please contact the box office (937-592-9002; office@thehollandtheatre.net) with questions or to have misplaced tickets re-issued.

item.title: 3 Essentials to Substantially Increase Your Business Growth
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: 3 Essentials to Substantially Increase Your Business Growth – Schedule an Initial Growth Consultation - Bellefontaine About this event What’s included in the initial growth consultation: -Review your current growth approach and overview how an effective, measurable sales campaign will benefit your business -Discuss your ideal customer and how you can increase prospect connections and increase customers won -Review the facility resources, digital marketing tools, and sales growth support that is included in our plans Who is the best fit for our program: -A business that sells time, skill & knowledge (less ideal are retail/restaurant) -Desire to grow significantly and desire some help to reach that goal -Don’t currently have a sales office or an effective sales campaign for the -Columbus area (work from home, the job site or are located away from their Columbus customers) Types of Entrepreneurs We Frequently Serve: -Training, consulting, and personal coaches that are paid for their knowledge and process. -Contractors and skilled trades that need a business address and space for office tasks and team meetings. -Small creative, technology businesses that need to collaborate with their team and clients. -Nonprofits and social enterprises that need a remote office or have a lean team and facility budget. -Financial planners, wealth managers, CPAs, and bookkeepers that need a private place to meet with clients.

item.title: Jack & Joe Waters
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Jack & Joe Waters Take The Stage For A Night You Won't Forget! Music, Beer, and Funnel Cakes! A collaboration you didn't know you needed until now! General admission tickets are $12.00 to get in the door. VIP tickets are $22.00 and include Beer + Funnel Cake + Concert! Check in at The Olive Tree to recieve your beer and funnel cake ticket to present to Roundhouse Depot and Freyhof's Funnel Cakes. Take advantage of the DORA weekend and enjoy your choice of Beer, Wine, or Cider from Roundhouse Depot and walk it back to The Olive Tree. Doors will open at 7:30pm to get checked in and claim your VIP Tickets. Jack and Joe Waters are a Father and Son duo based out of Dayton, Ohio. Jack started playing guitar when he was in the fifth grade. Joe got his start playing when he was around five years old. They have traveled around the country playing folk and jazzy blues music. They have won four local blues challenges that have made them semi finalists in the memphis international blues competition. After playing together for 17+ years , Jack and Joe are seasoned sounding professionals. You can feel every last note they put into the music. You will not be disappointed.

item.title: St. Patrick's Day Celebration
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: We will be tapping our Irish Red Ale on March 17th.We will continue the celebration into Friday with Schmidt Sausage Food Truck serving 5 to 8pm. Come celebrate with us. Cheers

item.title: Don McLean: 50th Anniversary "American Pie" Tour
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
American icon Don McLean takes the Holland stage as part of the Grammy winner's 50th Anniversary "American Pie" Tour.
One of the songs that helped define the 20th century, "American Pie" still goes to the heart as soon as McLean strums its opening notes. The singer-songwriter will also perform hits such as "Vincent (Starry Starry Night)," and "And I Love You So" as well as newer compositions.

item.title: Ebony & Ivory Dueling Pianos
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: This act features two entertainers that are playing the piano and singing while getting the crowd involved to sing, dance, and even have some fun roasting and who knows what-else these funny guys and gals have up their sleeves. It’s guaranteed to be a good time for all. As all attendees are encouraged to request their favorite songs. These entertainers have traveled around the country and entertained from cruise ships to casinos. You’re getting Top notch fun! You can throw any popular song at our dueling piano players, Country, Rap, Oldies, 80’s Rock, Blues, Dance and today’s Pop music from Bruno Mars, Taylor Swift it doesn’t matter. The show is designed for all age groups and environments. Doors open at 5:30pm and the show starts at 6:00pm. Small bites and cocktails will be available for purchase. For future events, visit syndicatedowntown.com

item.title: St. Patrick's Day & March Madness Kick-Off
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Kickoff of March Madness 🏀 AND St. Paddy’s Day 🍀 means HAPPY HOUR ALL NIGHT! Join us this Thursday for $3 craft beers and half off select appetizers, cocktails, bottles of wine, and more! Snag your table at bookthesyndicate.com 🥂

item.title: Easter EGG-stravaganza, Presented by Citizens Federal Savings & Loan
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us for Bellefontaine's premier spring event! Since it's Spring Break week, our fun activities will be happening ALL DAY LONG 🙌🏻 The Easter EGG-stravaganza is generously sponsored by Citizens Federal Savings & Loan and is sure to be fun for the whole family! We are working on finalizing the exact schedule, but think egg decorating, cookie frosting, craft making, egg & spoon racing, animal petting and movie watching! We will be posting the final schedule of events in just a few days, so check back! A portion of Main Street will shut down at 4:30pm to make way for food trucks, a DJ, a chalk activity & a petting zoo! The Easter Bunny will be at The Photo Booth for photos from 4-8pm. Get your photo shoot and a SWAG bag for only $30! Tickets on sale at Eventbrite. In the event of inclement weather, this event will be moved to Saturday, April 2.

item.title: B-Town BrewFest, presented by Royer Realty & Alan Galvez Insurance
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: B-Town Brew Fest is back and we’re brewing up some fun in Downtown Bellefontaine this May! This event is brought to you by our friends over at Alan Galvez Insurance & Royer Realty. THE DEETS: Mark your calendar for May 6th from 5-8pm. You will 10 tasting tickets at check-in to use throughout the downtown to sample a variety of Ohio made beer. Four Acre Clothing will be on-site screen printing special edition shirts. Downtown will be sprinkled with food trucks and plenty of activities to participate in along your tasting route. Did someone say silent disco?! Giant inflatable Hungry, Hungry, Hippos? YES PLEASE. GENERAL ADMISSION TICKETS: Purchase of a general admission ticket will land you 10 tasting tickets, each redeemable for 5 ounces of beer at designated stops, a commemorative fanny pack, and a sturdy, commemorative, reusable solo cup. DESIGNATED DRIVER TICKETS: All the merch perks of general admission without the extra cost of alcohol! New this year, an NA beverage on us. VIP TICKETS: In addition to your 10 tasting tickets, commemorative fanny pack, and commemorative reusable solo cup, you will also gain admission to our exclusive afterparty at The Syndicate. Your first drink is on us while you enjoy live music! MISC INFORMATION: Check-in will be 4:30-6:30pm at Roundhouse Brewing Depot. All Bellefontaine First Friday events are brought to you by our friends at Mary Rutan Hospital. All ticket sales are final. Must be 21 or older to participate. ID MUST BE PRESENTED AT CHECK-IN. WITHOUT THE PRESENCE OF YOUR ID (regardless of age and who you might know that's a volunteer), ACCESS WILL BE DENIED TO THIS EVENT.

item.title: Brewfontaine Welcomes Sonder Brewing
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us as we welcome Mason Ohio’s own Sonder Brewing. Sonder Brewing crafts high quality beer with a mix of story and community. Complimentary logo glassware available while supplies last! FEATURED DRAFT You Betcha ! Hazy IPA 6.5% A hoppy, hazy, tropical fruit explosion with a juicy and soft texture leaving bitterness behind Record Hop Vol 7 IPA 6.8% Record Hop Vol 7 is a collaboration with Yakima Chief Hops, a 100% grower-owned network of family hop farms who we partner with for the hops we use in our beers. Volume 7 features experimental hops from Yakima Chief Hops. The hops are HBC 586 & YCH 602 Trial Hops Blanc Wheat Beer 5.4% Blanc is sessionable, fruity, and crisp Grand Reserve Blend One Imperial Stout 12% Grand Reserve Blend One chocolate stout rested for 13 months in decades old Kentucky Wheated Bourbon Barrels. The beer beams with brown sugar, sticky maple syrup, caramel, and sweet cream. Blend One proceeds across your pallet with light molasses, chocolate, hints of nuts, earthy spicy oak notes, and ends with a tantalizing finish. Rally Cap Lager 5.2% A traditional German Lager brewed with Weyermann Malt from Germany, delicately hopped, and fermented cold. For future events, visit brewfontaine.com

item.title: Downtown Bellefontaine Spring Cleaning
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Join the Downtown Bellefontaine community to celebrate Earth Day and clean up our downtown before we head into spring and summer.
Volunteers will get a free lunch, t-shirt, and one complimentary refreshment from Brewfontaine after the event!
Following the clean up day, Anytime Fitness will host a community clean up Saturday morning following their 8 am community workout.
Sign up by Friday, April 15 to be guaranteed a t-shirt.

item.title: Unlocked with Blonde Vanity
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: 18 & up for this event 18-20 will have a $20 Door Charge 21 & up will be a $10 Door Charge Come out & join us for the 1st Drag Queen show with the lovely Blonde Vanity as your host. Let's show these Queens some love

item.title: Home Sweet Home Grand Reunion
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Join The Syndicate for a multi-class BHS reunion sponsored by the Class of ’92 and Bent Water Brewing.
Opens at 4 pm, price includes bottomless Bent Water Beer and Seltzer, BBQ. Bands include acoustics by Shane Fullerton and bands SaMax and The Fabulous Johnson Brothers.
100% of proceeds going to the BHS Athletic Department in memory of our close friends who are no longer with us.
Limited tickets are available for this private event and is age 21+.

item.title: Rumours Live
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
This cover band expertly recreates Fleetwood Mac’s signature harmonies, instrumentation, stage moves and wardrobe, keeping alive the legendary music for a devoted fan base. Rumours is led by Mekenzie Jackson and her effortless duplication of Steve Nicks’ iconic voice.
Based in Atlanta, Rumours has become one of the most sought after and highly regarded tribute bands in the genre. They attribute their success to their genuine passion for the music and magic of Fleetwood Mac.
Tickets go on sale Monday May 2, 2022 at 1:00 PM

item.title: Zane Gerlach Live!
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Live music with Zane Gerlach in our Beer Garden from 7-10pm. Free admission for all live music!
Enter in at the Beer Garden Entrance on the side of The Syndicate building if you are looking to attend our live music. If you are looking for regular dinner service, we would suggest booking a reservation on our website to guarantee a table inside of our restaurant.
Tables are first come, first serve in the beer garden.
If you are at a table, you will have a server for food and drinks. If you don't have a table, you can still order from food and drinks from our mobile bar.
Outside food and alcohol is not permitted into the premises. There are designated smoking areas outside of the fencing area, but smoking and vaping are not permitted inside the fenced areas.
In case of inclement weather, the event may be canceled or rescheduled.
For parking, please review our parking map at https://syndicatedowntown.com/parking-map/
Should you have any questions, please reach out to events@syndicatedowntown.com

item.title: Ebony & Ivory Dueling Pianos
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
This act features two entertainers that are playing the piano and singing while getting the crowd involved to sing, dance, and even have some fun roasting and who knows what-else these funny guys and gals have up their sleeves.
It’s guaranteed to be a good time for all. As all attendees are encouraged to request their favorite songs. These entertainers have traveled around the country and entertained from cruise ships to casinos. You’re getting Top notch fun! You can throw any popular song at our dueling piano players, Country, Rap, Oldies, 80’s Rock, Blues, Dance and today’s Pop music from Bruno Mars, Taylor Swift it doesn’t matter.
The show is designed for all age groups and environments.
Doors open at 5:30pm and the show starts at 6:00pm. Small bites and cocktails will be available for purchase.
Tickets are not currently available for this event.

item.title: Comedy Night with the Queens (18+)
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Featuring The Roast of Blonde Vanity! (18+)
We all love Blonde Vanity so how about we read her to filth!!!! Blonde Vanity will be joined with some queens that have some words to say!
You don't want to miss this.
Doors open at 7:30 and show is 8-11pm
Limited tables and seating so come early for a seat!
BYOB or take advantage of DORA night in Bellefontaine and grab a drink from many of the local bars and come on over.
We recommend getting your tickets ahead of time or you can buy tickets at the door.
Bring your friends and get ready to laugh your ass off for Bellefontaine's BEST comedy night!
See you there!

item.title: Ladies First
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Windmill Productions presents this humorous look at what might have transpired if Jackie Kennedy had invited former First Ladies to the White House for tea and advice. Starring local women, this play is a gently comedic take on the highest unpaid job in the USA.

item.title: The Maine Event - Exclusive Tapping of Maine Dinner DIPA
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us on Thursday, April 28th from 5:00- 10:00 as we tap Maine Dinner for the first time. Out of every Maine Beer Company product, Dinner is the most coveted and rarely makes its way to Ohio. Whether or not you support this level of exclusivity and line-standing is another matter. But there’s no doubt that the 8.2% beer is one of the best IPAs in America. With a perfect score of 100 on beeradvocate.com and Ratebeer.com, this is surely one you won’t want to miss. DINNER DOUBLE INDIA PALE ALE ABV 8.2% Dinner is our very special double IPA made with 7 lbs of hops per barrel. Drink this beer fresh! Aromas of mango, passionfruit, and pineapple. Undertones of garlic, onion, and pine. Sweet aromatics of malt and caramel. Also Available LUNCH INDIA PALE ALE ABV 7% Lunch is a special whale that has been spotted off the Maine coast since 1982. She has what looks like a bite taken out of her fin, which adds to her unique character. We dedicate this beer to her determination and persistence. Keep on swimming! Aromas of orange, grapefruit, and lemon with pine and herbal undertones. Tropical notes of guava and papaya with subtle caramel and malt sweetness. Wolfe's Neck INDIA PALE ALE ABV 6.5% A gift to the people of Maine and its visitors, the region known as Wolfe’s Neck reminds us of the importance of protecting land for future generations to experience and enjoy. Aromas of pineapple, mango, guava, coconut, and orange. Notes of onion and garlic. Underlying toasted bread and pine. Another One INDIA PALE ALE ABV 7% Aromas of pineapple, mango, and guava with notes of orange, grapefruit, and melon. Undertones of earthy, woody pine, and faint onion and garlic. for future events, please visit brewfontaine.com

item.title: May Paint n Sip
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: It's getting that time to be thinking about lake time and vacations. This month's painting is perfect for you lake-lovers. A tranquil sailboat ride soothes the mind. This painting will help put you in a calm mood while you are painting and imagining yourself on the water. Bring a friend and a beverage. Adult beverages are always welcome. Fee: $40

item.title: Pineapple-Palooza, Presented by Four Acre Clothing Company
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us this June for a summer festival you'll never forget! Bounce on inflatables, play games, and ZIP LINE DOWN MAIN STREET! This event will also feature food trucks, music, and a craft & vendor show! Pineapple Palooza is generously sponsored by Four Acre Clothing Company, Hi-Point Graphics, and All-Around Awards. Tickets will be sold onsite for the Zip Line as well as to participate in the other large attractions! Have some fun for only $1. A detailed schedule of events is coming soon! Amp up the fun by also purchasing Brew Fest tickets: https://www.eventbrite.com/e/305914567707

item.title: Art Journal Workshop
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Art journaling is a great way to express yourself, de-stress and be creative. In this workshop, you will start with creating your cover, then learn a few techniques for your pages. I will have a table full of great materials and supplies to use on your cover and pages. You can then bind your book with rings or ribbon. There will be plenty of pages for you to continue your journal after the workshop. This is NOT a do as I do workshop. I will give you a foundation to build upon. You do you! You are welcome to stay longer than the 2 hours allotted for this class if you find it as addictive as I do. 10% off your supply purchases during workshop.

item.title: Butterfly Flutters Paint n Sip
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: These butterfly paintings are a beautiful addition to any home decor. Their 8"x8" size makes them versatile; hang them on the wall or display them on a table or mantle sitting on an easel. Don't worry, Marilyn will have them sketched out and will walk you through step-by-step. As always, bring your favorite beverage. Fee- $40

item.title: South Pacific
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Windmill Productions presents this beloved Rodgers and Hammerstein musical, with songs including “Some Enchanted Evening,” “I’m Gonna Wash That Man Right Outa My Hair,” and “I’m in Love With a Wonderful Guy.”
Set on a South Pacific island during World War II, the musical’s plot centers on an American nurse who falls in love with a middle-aged expatriate French plantation owner but struggles to accept his mixed-race children. A secondary romance takes place between a U.S. Marine lieutenant and a young Tonkinese woman.
Tickets are $25 for adults and $5 for youth 17 and under for most seats. Come be transported during this evening of unforgettable musical theater!

item.title: Children's Summer Theater Workshop
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Windmill Productions and the Holland Theatre are excited to be working again with Compass Creative Dramatics to offer a summer theater opportunity for children entering grades 1-6. The week's events include a workshop July 11-15 and performances July 15 and 16 of a Beauty and the Beast-themed show. As in the past, registration is on a first-come, first-served basis. Cost is $35/child and includes theatrical instruction Monday-Friday from 9 a.m. to 12:15 p.m. by two professional actor/educators, a role in the selected play, and performances for a public audience at 7 p.m. Friday, July 15, and Saturday, July 16 (time TBD). Please note that Compass Creative’s Covid protocols will apply. For more information and registration materials, contact the Holland at office@thehollandtheatre.net. Tickets for this production will go on sale to the public at a later date.

item.title: Bourbon Club - Woodford Reserve
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us for a food and bourbon pairing evening in June in our outdoor Beer Garden. Bourbons: Woodford reserve, Woodford Double oaked, Woodford Malt, Woodford Rye General Admission Ticket: General admission will get you in for the bourbon and food pairing. Doors open at 6pm. VIP Experience: This experience starts prior to our tasting and will start at 5:15 and ending at 6:00pm. Doors open at 5pm. After your VIP experience, you will be seated with general admission guests to finish the night with bourbon and food pairing. This events is age 21+ and tickets are limited. For future events, please visit syndicatedowntown.com/events

item.title: Community Carnival
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Community Carnival When: Saturday, June 11, 3:00-7:00 pm Where: Brown Park, Bellefontaine, Ohio Cost: FREE Calvary Baptist Church is hosting a family friendly event that will take families through a series of interactive presentations that tell of the Good News of Jesus Christ. Families will then be invited to grab some food (hot dogs, chips, popsicles, and water), then enjoy various carnival style games and bounce houses. Any questions, please contact Calvary Baptist Church at 937-599-4775

item.title: Crawfish Boil
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us for our first ever crawfish boil presented by Cray Cray Cajun fresh crawfish flown in fro. New Orleans directly. Don't miss this boil.

item.title: 2022 Crawfish Boil feat. Branch & Bone Artisan Ales
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: We are very excited to partner Branch & Bone Artisan Ales for our 2022 Crawfish boil! Branch & Bone Artisan Ales was born out of friendship and a passion for beer. What began as a hobby has developed into a love for beer and the community that shares this appreciation. From there, the curiosity and exploration of alternative brewing and fermentation techniques took hold. A desire to create a balanced and flavorful product at the highest quality possible while contributing our passion to the community that surrounds us has led to the founding of our brewery. Our Branch & Bone draft line up for this year’s event will be: Little Cloud NEIPA 5.5% This little IPA is brewed exclusively with Mosaic hops and features tropical and stone fruit notes with an overlying dank quality Beach Bum Sour Ale 4% Fruited Joose Berliner featuring insane volumes of Pomegranate, Tangerine, and Pink Guava. This intensely fruited sour will make you feel like you you are on the beach with no cares left. Prepare The Ground Stout Never before released. Info TBA. Gorlami Italian Pilsner 5.5% Italian style pilsner. An emerging style of beer, Gorlami is a lager brewed with pilsner malt and noble hops, dry hopped with Saphir hops. This bright, crispy, floral hop forward pilsner was made for warmer weather. The all-you-can-eat menu will consist of: - Crawfish Boil - Fresh Louisiana Crawfish (delivered LIVE the day of the event) - Andouille Sausage - Shrimp - Corn on the Cob - Onion and Red Potatoes - Collard Greens w/ Pancetta - Jalapeño Cheddar Corn Bread w/ Old Bay Compound Butter. All tickets are general admission and includes the menu above, a Branch & Bone logo beer glass, live music from Hebdo, and your first beer. Not to mention access to private party in the Syndicate beer garden! 7Thirty8 Apparel will be on site live printing event tee shirts from their mobile print truck. This event is 21+ Seating is all first come first serve *Should the event need to be moved due to weather, the event will be moved to June 22 and will be announced 48 hours in advance. For future events, please visit syndicatedowntown.com

item.title: Trivia
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Trivia night is Back. Grab your friends and enjoy a night of brain teasers.

item.title: Roundhouse 4 Year Anniversary Party
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: 4 year Anniversary weekend of fun Friday night Flying Pepper Food Truck 4 to 9pm Live Music SaMax 6 to 9 pm Saturday R Smokehouse 11-1p Hot Mess Food Truck 5 -9p 7thirty8 apparel printing 5-8p Music Two for the Road 6-9p New Beer Releases

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: JUL 8 AT 4 PM – JUL 9 AT 10 PM

item.title: Creating Curb Appeal for Your Business
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: How can your storefront draw customers and clients in? What makes a potential customer stop in front of YOUR location and say, "I need to go in here!" - and how do you continue to cultivate that sense of curiosity and intrigue? Join us for a presentation and discussion with the team from Revival Design Studio on the best ways to attract customers using the biggest, boldest tool you have: your storefront! We'll have some great giveaways for attendees to update their storefronts so be sure to join us!

item.title: Toxic Brew/Brewfontiane Collab Can Release: Jalle’ Berry
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: It's the most wonderful time of the year! What started as Fitz's home-brew has now become an annual tradition as we collaborate with our pals at Toxic Brew Co. in Dayton on this raspberry jalapeño pale ale. It has a sweet nose with hints of pepper. The flavor is full of jalapeño and soft raspberry sweetness. It finishes nice and dry with a pleasant pepper flavor and subtle spiciness. We have a limited supply of four packs of 16oz cans available as well as a limited edition 2021 Jalle' Berry t-shirts, featuring artwork by the one and only Spencer Razinger. Featured drafts include Jalle’ Berry (OG), Blackberry variant, habanero variant, as well as some other goodies from Toxic Brew Co.

item.title: Savage Race After Party
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Come to Brewfontaine after the Savage Race for the After Party! Any person wearing their t-shirt or medal will receive a 15% discount. We look forward to seeing you there! For future events, please see us at brewfontaine.com

item.title: Summer Sidewalk Sales
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us in Downtown Bellefontaine for Summer Sidewalk Sales and save big! Many stores will have massive sales inside and outside of their stores - pick up a passport at any participating location and collect stamps to win an amazing prize basket! This will also be a DORA weekend, meaning you can grab a drink at any downtown bar and carry it around with you downtown as you shop. Most participating stores will be open at least 10am-6pm on Friday, but their hours all vary so check with your favorites before you head out! Shopping passports will list the open hours of all businesses for easy navigation that weekend. Make a dinner reservation and join us in Downtown Bellefontaine!

item.title: Bubbles, Brews & Bags
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Women United of Logan County is hosting Bubbles, Bags & Brews Tuesday, July 26 from 5-7 pm. Join us at Roundhouse Depot Brewing Company for food, friendship and fun during this FREE event! Light refreshments will be provided. Regular and specialty drinks will be available for purchase. Bring a new or gently used purse/clutch/handbag to donate for an upcoming event and your first drink is on us! All current and NEW members will receive raffle tickets for fun prizes, too! Women United membership is not necessary, but we would LOVE for you to consider joining this United Way of Logan County outreach program!

item.title: Dr. Seuss Brunch at The Fun Company!
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us as we celebrate Dr. Seuss with a FUN themed brunch at The Fun Company! We'll be serving up Green Eggs & Ham as well as a variety of yummy breakfast treats. Crafts & activities provided too! Tickets are $10 each and breakfast time slots are available at 10am and 11am, with a special guest reading to us at 11! Grab your tickets and make plans to join us! Please note the time on your ticket as breakfast will be served in two shifts. We cant wait to see you!

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: The Fun Company, home of Education Station

item.title: Art & Wine Walk, Presented by Six Hundred Downtown
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us in Downtown Bellefontaine for a summer night you'll never forget! The Art & Wine Walk, presented by Six Hundred Downtown is always a crowd favorite. Artists and food trucks will line the streets, with interactive art opportunities for kids and grownups alike! Your ticket also allows you to sample 8 wines out of a keepsake glass engraved locally by All-Around Awards. Tickets are only $25! Nab yours today. See less

item.title: B-Town's Got Talent
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: All Acts Welcome! Registration Is Required To Be A Part Of The Show! Registration Deadline July 25th! Spectators encouraged to come watch! No cover charge for spectators. Featuring a Guest Celebrity judges panel! Prizes! And More! Registration is $10 per act. You will be given up to 5 minutes to impress the judges and receive up to a 10 point score per judge. Act with the highest score wins! Prizes and more details coming soon. There will be a kids competition 17 years old and under happening on our patio and an 18 and over competition happening on our main stage. First come first served! Do you have what it takes to win B-Town's Got Talent!?

item.title: Beauty and the Beast
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Local children take the stage at the end of a summer theater workshop from Windmill Productions and Compass Creative. This upbeat hour-long musical is loosely based on the classic tale of Beauty and the Beast.
