item.title: FREE Community Cowork Day
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Working from home? Why not try something different. BUILD is a great place to work and network with other business professionals. We have high speed fiber optic internet, comfortable working spaces and a roof top patio. Come in and see what we have to offer you, we know you'll love it!

item.title: Bourbon Club: High Bank Distillery￼
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Join us for our High Banks bourbon and cigar event in August. This event has a four course food pairing with four bourbons. You are able to purchase more cigars this evening should you choose.

Bourbons: High Banks Whiskey War, Barrel Proof, Double Oak, and Midnight Cask
General Admission Ticket (with cigar): General admission will get you in for the bourbon and food pairing and will also get you 1 cigar with your ticket. Doors open at 6pm. Enjoy being outside in our Beer Garden for an after event with a cigar and drink menu extended to be served through 9pm.

General Admission Ticket (no cigar): General admission will get you in for the bourbon and food pairing for dinner with the event completing at 8pm.

VIP Experience (with cigar): The VIP experience starts prior to our tasting and consists of a small plate and a VIP bourbon tasting at our bar. This starts at 5:15 and ending at 6:00pm. Doors open at 5pm. After your VIP experience, you will be seated with general admission guests to finish the night with bourbon, food pairing and cigar. Enjoy being outside in our Beer Garden for an after event with a cigar and drink menu extended to be served through 9pm.

VIP Experience (no cigar): The VIP experience starts prior to our tasting and consists of a small plate and a VIP bourbon tasting at our bar. This starts at 5:15 and ending at 6:00pm. Doors open at 5pm. After your VIP experience, you will be seated with general admission guests to finish the night with our bourbon and food pairing.

TIMELINE OF EVENTS

5:00PM Doors Open for VIP
5:15-5:45PM VIP Experience
5:45PM Doors Open for General Admission
6:00-8:00PM Event - Four Course Bourbon and Food Pairing
8:00-9:00PM After Party for Cigar Ticket Holders (Beer Garden)
This events is age 21+ and tickets are limited.

item.title: Amateur Cornhole Tournament￼
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Join us in Downtown Bellefontaine for an afternoon of cornhole and food/drink specials at The Syndicate!

Cash Prizes & Door Prizes!

OPEN TO ANYONE AND EVERYONE!

2 Round Robin, BYOP, double elimination, single players will be matched with other singles. Boards open at 2:30pm and tournament at 3pm.

Pre-register available thru Scoreholio:
https://app.scoreholio.com/link/Rm5r

item.title: Community Blood Drive
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Hickory Medical DPC is hosting a blood drive by Community Blood Center on Tuesday, August 23rd, between 12:30 pm and 3:30 pm. This is open to anyone eligible to donate and will take place in Lot 3, across the street from the Bellefontaine Post Office. You must register for the blood drive online, using the Community Blood Center website. Lite refreshments will be provided.

If you don't have a table, you can still order from food and drinks from our mobile bar. Outside food and alcohol is not permitted into the premises. There are designated smoking areas outside of the fencing area, but smoking and vaping are not permitted inside the fenced areas. In case of inclement weather, the event may be canceled or rescheduled. For parking, please review our parking map at https://syndicatedowntown.com/parking-map/ Should you have any questions, please reach out to events@syndicatedowntown.com For additional events, please visit syndicatedowntown.com/events

item.title: Po' Ramblin' Boys
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Enjoy a night of energetic traditional bluegrass with these Grammy-nominated, hard-driving favorites. Based in Tennessee’s Smoky Mountains, the Po’ Ramblin’ Boys got their start at a local moonshine distillery. Over the years of touring and recording, the quintet has built a devoted fan base. As a Journal of Roots Music review put it, "they take the old and make it new, while taking the new and rendering it timeless.” Doors open at 6:45.

item.title: The Band of Heathens with Dalton Domino
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Described by Rolling Stone as “a smoking live band,” these indie favorites have released nine acclaimed albums of roots rock originals over the past 15 year and toured the world many times over. Their latest album, Remote Transmissions: Vol. 1, takes its inspiration from a segment of a Zoom program the band hosted during the pandemic. The album features covers of ten classic songs with guest vocals by the likes of Margo Price, Ray Wylie Hubbard, and Charlie Starr. Americana singer-songwriter Dalton Domino opens.

item.title: Joe Troop and Dirk Powell
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Steeped in the music of their native Appalachia, these musicians both celebrate and enlarge the traditions that shaped them. Joe Troop, appearing as part of a quartet, is a multi-instrumentalist, singer, and songwriter. The founder of Grammy-nominated stringband Che Apalache, Troop brings a musical approach honed by both his upbringing in North Carolina and his international travels, including a decade living in Buenos Aires. Dirk Powell unites traditional fiddle and banjo playing with modern sensibilities, reflecting his more than nine generations of roots in the southern mountains. His music has also been featured in numerous films. He appears at the Holland as part of a hard-driving trio.

item.title: Julian Lage
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: One of the most prodigious guitarists of his generation and in the “highest category of improvising musicians" (New Yorker), Julian Lage has spent more than a decade drawing from myriad strains of American musical history via impeccable technique, free association, and a spirit of infinite possibility. Julian Lage is also thrilled to offer an exclusive Master Class at select cities on his View With A Room Fall Tour. All Master Class tickets include admission for one to the evening's concert. Unfortunately, we're unable to offer "master class-only tickets" at this time. Start time for the class is generally an hour before advertised doors but exact start time will be communicated with ticket buyers around one week before the show. During each class, 30 minutes will be spent teaching to a unique piece of curriculum before a 10-15 minute Q&A session. Tickets to these classes are limited to ensure everyone comes away with a unique and hands-on experience. Participants are not required to bring instruments; topics are not necessarily tied to instruction and technique. Participants will also leave with an exclusive Julian Lage poster.

item.title: Jake Blount (Holland Backstage Series)
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Described by NPR as “an Afrofuturist in roots-music garb,” Blount is a skilled performer of spirituals, blues and string band repertoire. His 2020 album Spider Tales (named one of the year’s best albums by NPR and The New Yorker, with a 5-star review from The Guardian) highlighted the Black and Indigenous histories of American folk tunes. His Fall 2022 release, The New Faith, features radical arrangements of traditional songs as it answers the question, “What would Black music sound like after climate change renders most of the world uninhabitable? What gods would this community praise, and what stories would they tell?” This Backstage concert offers a unique, intimate experience, with both audience members and performers on the stage. Seating is general admission.

item.title: Connor - Roundhouse Brewery
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Connor Raines - acoustic come drink, eat and listen to amazing tunes

item.title: Art Around Town
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: If you had a blast at Pineapple Palooza you won't want to miss the First Friday in August! Join us Friday, August 5th from 5-9pm for an art festival to remember! Watch live music, artists and dancers perform. Take part in some amazing interactive art pieces for free! Watch the unveiling of Bellefontaine's newest mural at 6:30pm and stroll through the streets while taking in all of the sights and sounds. There will even be a talent show going on at The Olive Tree! Sign up here https://www.eventbrite.com/.../b-towns-got-talent-tickets... This event will also feature food trucks, music, and it's a DORA weekend! Art Around Town is running in conjunction with the Art & Wine Walk presented by Six Hundred Downtown and the Logan County Art League Street Faire. Tickets will be sold onsite for a Kids Palette Passport where they get to travel around town letting their creative juices flow! This passport will get your child several amazing art projects that they can take home with them! But there will also be some amazing free art to be a part of! A detailed schedule of events is coming soon! Amp up the fun by also purchasing Art and Wine tickets: https://www.eventbrite.com/e/365645303947

item.title: Plant & Pottery Swap
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: MARK YOUR CALENDARS PLANT PEOPLE! Sunday, August 14th, 12p.m.-4p.m. Beck Plant Haus is hosting a plant and pottery swap at the Café area in Main Street Marketplace! You have one month to get some propagations going! It will be really fun to meet in one place and see what plants everyone is into! And... no one said you couldn't bring one just to show off 😜 *FREE EVENT. NO MONEY EXCHANGED FOR PLANTS OR POTS. *DORA Approved (I will not be selling DORA cups, but if another vendor is, feel free to bring them in with you!

item.title: Hot Summer Nights Cruise In
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Registration and parade from fairgrounds. Parade at 5:15 to downtown. DJ, food vendors and great classics

item.title: Charcuterie Board and Wine Tasting Class
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
DATE NIGHT AT BUILD
Looking for something fun to do for Friday date night, or with a friend? Of course, singles are welcome!
We have the answer and we're positive you'll LOVE IT!
BUILD is having Graze Local come in to teach you how to create a charcuterie board pleasing to both your taste buds and your eyes. Your board will include 4 different cheeses, jam, meat, seasonal fruit, nuts and crackers. Graze Local will also pair wine with the variety of cheeses you will be adding to your board.
You can enjoy your board here OR you can take your bamboo charcuterie board home with you.
Keep the board as our gift to you and you can entertain your friends and family again and again.
Tickets are $70 per couple. One ticket will cover up to two people.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Charcuterie Board and Wine Tasting Class

item.title: Toastmasters Learning Luncheon
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
DON'T MISS our September Learning Luncheon with our local Toastmasters.
*Whether you are speaking at a large conference or to your supervisor, the secret is in the preparation.
* Toastmasters will teach you how to structure your words in an inviting format. Provide examples of how to open your presentation to catch the audience's attention and how to close your presentation to include your major points.
*You will also learn how to control the jitters when addressing a crowd
Tickets are $20 per person and will include a box lunch from the Flying Pepper.

Aurora Photos LLC will be at BUILD on 9/12 and 9/16 from 9am-2pm! You can take your photos outside, inside BUILD, or use the formal backdrop provided! It’s extremely important to ”put a face with the name” - so if you already have a small business, are starting a small business, or work in a Professional field within our small community, this is a perfect opportunity to create headshots that can be used for several marketing purposes! item.dc:creator: admin_sn

We will be serving up a Dr. Seuss themed menu and two very special guests, Thing 1 and Thing 2, will be paying us a visit to read their favorite book! Tickets are $10 each and include breakfast, drinks, and the special reading. Space is limited - reserve your seats today! Please call us if you have any questions :)

item.dc:creator: admin_sn

item.title: Thanksgiving Eve Bash with SaMax

item.title: Born in Ohio - Farm to Table Event
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Join us for our first ever Born in Ohio event at The Syndicate! Food and drink menu items have been special selected from vendors located in Ohio. This is a food and drink pairing event.
1st course: Deviled egg sampler 1 Bacon/Kimchee, 1 Maple Bourbon Salmon, and 1 Caper/Dill paired with Shoo-Wee Randy Shandy
2nd course: Jumbo shrimp, elotes corn salad, served over a corn cake paired with Pinot Grigio
3rd course: Farm beef brisket served over a warm fingerling potato salad, chimicurri, and blistered tomatoes paired with High Bank Bourbon
4th course: Chocolate Ganache garnished with a buckeye from Marie's Candies paired with a Watershed Vodka cocktail

item.title: The Pumpkin Walk, Presented by Small Nation
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Join us for one of the best nights of the year in Downtown Bellefontaine! This year the downtown will transform into a HAUNTED HOLLYWOOD, full of spooky surprises and more attractions than ever before! This year we're adding bounce houses, food trucks, and a whole lot more.
While exploring downtown during this event is FREE like always (including a free showing of The Great Pumpkin at The Holland at 7pm!), we highly recommend purchasing a passport for an upgraded experience!
For only $20, your child will get unlimited bounces on all of our inflatable attractions as well as a free mini session with Susie at The Photo Booth! We're also going to throw in a FREE pass for our caramel apple bar. What a delicious deal! Limited passports will be available for purchase at the door, so we highly recommend making your reservation in advance: https://www.eventbrite.com/e/410655300057
Are you interested in being a vendor at this event? Sign up here: https://www.eventbrite.com/e/410636905037

item.title: The Andrews Brothers￼
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Mistaken identities and madcap adventures abound when Windmill Productions presents this laughter-filled valentine to the music and heroes of World War II. A USO show is threatened with cancellation when a certain famous trio of singing sisters fails to show, and it’s up to three earnest and determined stagehands to go on with the show! The Andrews Brothers includes more than 25 songs made famous by the Andrews Sisters, including "Boogie Woogie Bugle Boy," "Slow Boat to China," "Don't Sit Under the Apple Tree," and "Ac-Cent-Tchu-Ate the Positive."
Performances at 7:30 p.m. Saturday, Oct. 8, and 2 p.m. Sunday, Oct. 9.

item.title: Garrison Keillor & Company￼
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
PLEASE NOTE: THIS SHOW HAS BEEN RESCHEDULED FROM AN EARLIER DATE. TICKETS WITH THE ORIGINAL DATE ARE STILL VALID, WITH SEAT ASSIGNMENTS INTACT.

Garrison Keillor is best known for his forty years with the beloved radio show “A Prairie Home Companion.” He wrote fiction and comedy and invented a town called Lake Wobegon, where all the children are above average. In retirement, he has written a memoir and a book of limericks and is at work on a musical and a Lake Wobegon screenplay, and he continues “The Writers Almanac,” sent out daily to internet subscribers. He appears with Prudence Johnson and Dan Chouinard for an evening of poetry, classic love songs, the news from Lake Wobegon, and a discussion of why you should keep getting older.

item.title: Bobby Floyd Trio
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Grammy nominee and Emmy Award winner Bobby Floyd is known internationally for his skill on the piano, organ, and keyboards. Popular at home in Columbus, where he plays with the Columbus Jazz Orchestra and other groups, he has also performed with other distinguished orchestras and has toured extensively accompanying luminaries such as Ray Charles. He is joined by Derek DiCenzo on bass and guitar and Reggie Jackson on drums, who have also traveled and played with award-winning musicians including Diane Schuur.

item.title: Martin Sexton￼
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Singer-songwriter Martin Sexton released his tenth studio album this year and is touring with what Rolling Stone calls his “soul-marinated voice,” acoustic guitar, and a suitcase full of heartfelt songs. The latest release (2020 Vision) is produced by three-time Grammy-nominee John Alagia with John Mayer guesting on guitar. Headlining venues from The Fillmore to Carnegie Hall, Sexton has influenced a generation of contemporary artists. His songs have appeared in television series and numerous films, but it's his incendiary live show, honest lyrics, and vocal prowess that keep fans coming back for a new experience every time.

item.title: Murder Mystery Drag Show
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Bill Kruse tells a story of our very own Tyler Berry! Which queen did it? This interactive and hilarious show will be The Olive Tree's FIRST original production! Follow Tyler as he goes through his day and runs into the craziest, mysterious, and deadliest queens! Who did it? Interact with the cast and place your vote on who you think murdered Tyler!
This show will still be classic drag show style, so bring your dollar bills, come ready for a cocktail, and be prepared to laugh all night long!
Limited seating $5 presale $10 at the door +18

item.title: Block Party with Maybe Cheese Born With It!
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Come celebrate with us as we raise a toast to being the first open and proud gay bar in Bellefontaine, OH! Party The Olive Tree way as we shut down 2 streets and have a big ole block party!
Spend the whole day shopping 40+ artists and merchants, or exploring Downtown Bellefontaine! - - - - - Traveling from Toledo, Ohio, help us welcome Maybe Cheese Born With It! This mac and cheese food stand came to fame by adding a twist on the food truck scene, Drag Queen Style! Not only is the mac and cheese good it's the BEST! As seen on Food Network's The Great Food Truck Race, Maybe Cheese Born With It landed second in the competition! We couldn't be more excited to bring them to Bellefontaine!

item.title: Kennedy Davenport at The Olive Tree
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: The dancing diva of Texas is coming to Bellefontaine, OH! The RuPaul's Drag Race season 7 and RPDR All Stars season 3 queen is taking The Olive Tree stage! GA $15 (Standing Room) 9pm VIP EXPERIENCE $30 (Standing Room) 8pm VIP TABLE $200 (Seats 4) 8pm

item.title: House of Horrors with Leota Tombs
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Leota Tombs Returns and Brings Talent and Nightmares! A show you do not want to miss! This will be the official Halloween Party at The Olive Tree! Dressing up is encouraged! Plan to see specialty one night only spooky themed cocktails! Cover charge is $5.00 ahead and $8.00 at the door!

item.title: Lunch & Learn: Conflict Resolution￼
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us for lunch and a guest speaker from the Logan County sheriffs office to talk about conflict resolution in any situation!

item.title: Whiskeys Around the World
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Join us for Whiskeys Around the World in October. This event has a four course food pairing with four bourbons.

﻿Whiskeys: Glendronach, Jack Daniels Single Barrel, Slane Irish Whiskey, Benriach

1st course: Glendronach (Scotland)
﻿﻿Brisket Sliders, smoked brisket, goat cheese and chive mousse, pickled red onions and cucumbers, microgreens on toasted slider bun.

2nd course: Jack Daniels Single Barrel (Tennessee)
﻿﻿Butternut squash ravioli with crispy pork belly, pine nuts, grilled asparagus pieces, tossed in a sage brown butter sauce, finished with pecorino.

3rd course: Slane Irish Whiskey (Ireland)
﻿﻿Guinness Beef Stew, braised beef, carrots, celery, onion, herbs, garlic, served on a bed of horseradish mashed potatoes, finished with crispy onion straws.

4th course: Benriach (Scotland)
﻿﻿Warm Apple Crisp, with ginger and oatmeal crumble, topped with churro ice cream from Whit’s.

General Admission Ticket: General admission will get you in for the whiskey and food pairing for dinner with the event completion at 8pm.

VIP Experience: The VIP experience starts prior to our event and consists of a small plate and a VIP bourbon tasting at our bar. This starts at 5:15 and ending at 6:00pm. Doors open at 5pm. After your VIP experience, you will be seated with general admission guests to finish the night with our whiskey and food pairing.

TIMELINE OF EVENTS
5:00PM Doors Open for VIP
5:15-5:45PM VIP Experience
5:45PM Doors Open for General Admission
6:00-8:00PM Event - Four Course Whiskey and Food Pairing

This events is age 21+ and tickets are limited.

item.title: Roundhouse Depot/Brewfontaine Collab Release: Ichabod Train
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Stop in during the Downtown Pumpkin walk and try Ichabod Train, an exclusive collaboration with Brewfontaine and Roundhouse Depot Brewing Co!
This Pumpkin Pie cream ale is loaded with pumpkin and spices, perfect for the season. Both locations will start pouring at 4:00 pm. Be sure to grab a DORA cup of Ichabod Train and walk the town to check out the festivities!!

item.title: Succulent Bar: Make & Take Event
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: We are excited to collaborate with our friends from Beck Plant Haus! Join us at unboxed for a fun make and take event. Purchase any in-stock mug from unboxed during this event. Then, choose from the variety of plants and succulents Beck Plant Haus has on hand in our shop. They will help you plant your own succulent on-site. We will have plenty of adorable succulents to choose from, and you know we have the best mugs around. Our teams are excited to help you create your own masterpiece

item.title: Winter Wreath Class
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Exercise your artistic mind!
Dorcey's Flowers will help you create your own unique, fresh Winter Wreath for the Holidays. Don't miss out, last year's class was a HUGE success.
Kristie from Dorcey's will be teaching us how to design and fashion a beautiful wreath that will last through the holiday season and beyond. What a great gift for yourself, your friends or family!
All supplies will be provided. Cost is $85 per person
Coffee, tea and snacks will be served during the class.

item.title: Todd Snider with Tommy Prine
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: The Holland is delighted to welcome back singer-songwriter Todd Snider, who played for an enthusiastic audience in November of 2021. This roots troubadour has released many acclaimed albums, but is best known for his live shows -- as captured on his latest release, Live: Return of the Troubador. Fan appreciate the meandering charm and deadly humor of both his songs and his inimitable storytelling. Opening will be Tommy Prine, son of the late legendary John Prine.

item.title: Brunch and Brews
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us for Brunch and Brews with Rise Bakehouse together we are hosting a great brunch experience. We will have fun Beer Cocktails to choose from. Beer Bloody Mary's Beer Mimosa Wine Cocktails.. No Tickets needed.

item.title: Chris Smither and Tim O'Brien with Jan Fabricius
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us for an unforgettable evening as acclaimed singer-songwriters and multi-instrumentalists bring their roots-influenced music to the Holland stage. Chris Smither has been creating acclaimed folk and blues-influenced music for more than four decades. As the Associated Press put it, “Smither is an American original – a product of the musical melting pot and one of the absolute best singer-songwriters in the world.” Also taking the stage will be Grammy winning singer-songwriter and multi-instrumentalist Tim O’Brien, a West Virginia native and lifelong devotee of old time and bluegrass music. He will be joined by his partner, Jan Fabricius, on harmony vocals and mandolin.

item.title: Windborne
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Windborne’s captivating show draws on the quartet’s deep roots in vocal harmony traditions, while their innovative arrangements bring old songs into the present. The ensemble shifts effortlessly between dramatically different styles of music, bringing audience members on a journey that spans continents and centuries and educates as it entertains. The group is also part of the long tradition of folk musicians championing the oppressed, poor, and disenfranchised. Committed to bringing vocal traditions to a younger audience, Windborne has found unexpected viral success on TikTok for such unlikely genres as Corsican polyphony or early 20th century labor anthems.

item.title: Blind Boys of Alabama Christmas Show
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Celebrate a joyful Christmas with these gospel legends! The Blind Boys of Alabama have the rare distinction of being recognized around the world as both living legends and modern-day innovators. They are not just gospel singers borrowing from old traditions; the group helped to define those traditions in the 20th century and almost single-handedly created a new gospel sound for the 21st. Since the original members first sang together as kids at the Alabama Institute for the Negro Blind in the late 1930s (including Jimmy Carter, who leads the group today), the band has become one of the most recognized and decorated roots music groups in the world.

item.title: Hoof Hearted Dragon Saddle Tapping & Cognac Barrel Giveaway
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Hoof Hearted brews Dragonsaddle just once a year and we scored a keg! Tapping at 5:00pm on Thursday 10/13. Come experience one of the Top 250 Beers in the World with this 11.5% Triple IPA! Also on tap will be “All My Friends Are Dead” , a Cognac/maple syrup barrel aged double mash imperial stout with HH coffee, hazelnut, vanilla and milk sugar. Enter the raffle for a chance to win the actual cognac barrel that it was aged in!! Other Hoof goodies will be available as well!!

item.title: WCOC Concert Band: FREE Holiday Concert￼
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Last year’s WCOC Concert Band holiday concert was such a hit that we are bringing the group back for what may become one of your family’s favorite Christmas traditions! Presented at no charge to the community, the evening will feature the sounds of the season and perhaps some special guest appearances. The WCOC Concert Band is celebrating its 30th season in 2022. Directed by Mr. Myles Bowers, the all-volunteer band include amateur musicians, professional musicians, educators, and students hailing from multiple Ohio counties. Seating will be on a first-come, first-served basis.

item.title: Christmas Movie with LCSO
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Come join LCSO staff for an afternoon movie. Santa will be making an appearance after the movie. This is a free community event.

item.title: The Bad Plus
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Now in their 21st year, The Bad Plus continues to push boundaries with founding members Reid Anderson on bass and Dave King drums along with Ben Monder on guitar and Chris Speed on tenor sax. Combining avant-garde jazz with rock and pop influences, the band’s repertoire includes songs from Queen’s “We Are the Champions” to jazz standard “My Funny Valentine,” as well as innovative originals.

item.title: The Elves and the Shoemaker
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Windmill Productions presents this charming retelling of the classic fairytale. Performed in musical style with a children’s chorus, The Elves and the Shoemaker’s timeless story of generosity and friendship is perfect for the holiday season. Performances at 7:30 p.m. Friday, Dec. 2, and 2 p.m. Saturday, Dec. 3.

item.title: One Night in Memphis
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: On Dec. 4, 1956, four giants of American music – Carl Perkins, Johnny Cash, Jerry Lee Lewis, and Elvis Presley – jammed at a Sun Records studio in Memphis. That unforgettable evening is re-created in this tribute starring former cast members of the Broadway smash Million Dollar Quartet and featuring more than 90 minutes of rockabilly, country, gospel, and 1950s rock and roll. Created and directed by John Mueller, One Night in Memphis will immerse audience members in the rollicking sounds of a bygone but timeless era of legendary performers.

item.title: Murder Mystery Dinner - Midnight at the Masquerade
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join The Syndicate for our first-ever murder mystery dinner! All ages are welcomed to enjoy a four course dinner as you waltz through this engaging experience to figure out who did it! The rustle of silks and feathers under muted conversation was probably the last thing heard by the tragically and unexpectedly deceased at the Billionaires’ Club Annual Masquerade Ball. With a mystery under each feathery façade, unmasking the killer may be quite a challenge. It’s up to you to determine which partygoer is guilty, bring this crime to justice, and save the party! Attend the Billionaires’ Club Annual Masquerade Ball and help get this soiree back on track by trading clues with your guests, gathering information, and solving the crime before the masked menace gets away! Although it isn't required, costumes are welcomed. Appropriate for the evening: evening wear, ball gowns, Venetian masks, suits, and tuxes are what to wear to this high class night of masks and murder. Get ready for a glamorous evening of mystery, intrigue, and murder at this masquerade ball murder mystery party!

item.title: Holiday Open House, Presented by Homegrown Yoga
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Our Holiday Open House weekend is BACK! With a twist this year - we're raising money to purchase Thanksgiving meals for area families in need through the Logan County Outreach Centers. Not only do you get to support a local family - but your $5 passport unlocks INCREDIBLE savings at over 20 downtown retailers! Your passport will be emailed to you by 10am on Friday, November 4th or you can pick it up that day at the Logan County Chamber of Commerce. Questions? Call Micky at 937-599-5121. T﻿hank you so much to Homegrown Yoga, the generous sponsor of this year's event!

item.title: Bellefontaine Best Hometown Celebration
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us in Downtown Bellefontaine to celebrate being named one of Ohio's Best Hometowns! This celebration on the Courthouse lawn, featuring special guests and an exciting presentation from Ohio Magazine, will be topped off with a performance by the Fountainaires! Food trucks will be present throughout the downtown. Each year, Ohio Magazine recognizes places that make our state a great place to live. To determine this year’s honorees, Ohio Magazine solicited nominations in the spring and conducted visits across the state over the summer. The magazine’s editors chose Bellefontaine for its impressive downtown redevelopment efforts, thriving sense of community and appreciation for preserving local history. While in downtown, enjoy incredible savings in downtown retailers during the annual First Friday's Holiday Open House going on all weekend. In case of inclement weather, the event will be held in the Holland Theater.

item.title: Pumpkin Carving Contest
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Calling all masters of the Gourd. Groove and Grave your way into the Brewfontaine record books in this year's Pumpkin Carving Contest. Entry is just $15 and includes 2 pints of Masthead Haunted Hay Ride and a slice of house made pumpkin cheesecake. Plus, growler specials for all attendees! THE RULES: Entrants can carve their pumpkins at home or at the restaurant. No carving tools will be provided. We're not responsible for any injuries you may or may not incur while creating your masterpiece, so carve carefully. There's no size or weight limit, but you must be able to move your pumpkin from the carving area to the judging area without assistance. Nothing that explodes or bursts into flames can be added to your pumpkin; everything else is fair game. TICKETS PURCHASED ARE FOR ENTRY ONLY, BUT TO COME AND VIEW IS FREE. PRIZES: Brewfontaine gift cards will be awarded to the 1st, 2nd, and 3rd placed pumpkins and the best Brewfontaine Themed Pumpkin. (See prizes below) $300 Gift Card | 1st Place $200 Gift Card | 2nd Place $100 Gift Card | 3rd Place $50 Gift Card | Brewfontaine Theme Schedule of Events: Contest opens at 5 p.m Public judging at 8 p.m This event is age 21+. For future events, please visit brewfontaine.com

item.title: Intro to Aerial Yoga
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Curious about aerial yoga? Want to learn more? This is the class for you! We will learn the basics of aerial yoga- fabric placement, how to get in and out of poses, and the benefits of aerial yoga. This is a longer time frame so that we have plenty of time to explain and explore the poses in a safe environment. You will feel confident and comfortable attending a future aerial class at Homegrown Yoga. Book through the MindBody App or www.homegrownyoga.fit Standard class pricing applies.

item.title: Kids Art and Yoga
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: This class is intended for any child ages 1-3 and the adult that accompanies them! Over the course of 90 minutes, we will explore yoga and art. The first part of class will be a yoga and mindfulness practice meant for big and little to do together. Connect with your little through movement! Following yoga, participants will complete an art project. The project this month will be a pumpkin patch painting. Crayola tempera paints will be used. The painting style is focused on letting the toddler feel the movement of art! They will create two 8"x8" canvas works of art. On one canvas the toddler will create a flowing background and add pumpkins with a sponge dabbler. On the second canvas, a larger pumpkin will be painted. The toddlers handprint will be added as the leaf. They will be booooootiful! Class is suitable for ages 1-3. An adult must be present the entire time. Personal investment: $35 Book through the Mindbody App, www.homegrownyoga.fit, or by calling 937-404-1464

item.title: Sound Bath with Anna
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: The sound of singing bowls is both captivating and calming and is frequently used as a meditation aid. The tones quickly instill a sense of trance-like calm, allowing listeners to drop into meditative states, and cultivate mindfulness and awareness in the present moment. The sound waves radiate outward from the bowls and wash over and through the listener. The physical body and the energy field of the listener enter into sympathetic resonance with this vibration. Each particle in the body is restored to their original vibration, frequency, and harmony. The healing effects of these bowls can be attributed to the special sound they emit as well as the conscious intention used in the process of their creation and during the session.

item.title: Full Moon Women's Circle
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join Anna for an evening of sisterhood, connection, and reflection. We will open with a brief meditation, journal together, and pull oracle cards from Anna’s very own deck, Animal Guides. You’ll leave feeling supported, guided, and held.

item.title: Full Moon Women's Circle

item.content:encoded: Join Anna for an evening of sisterhood, connection, and reflection. We will open with a brief meditation, journal together, and pull oracle cards from Anna’s very own deck, Animal Guides. You’ll leave feeling supported, guided, and held.

item.title: Mat Kearney: The Acoustic Trio Tour
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: With five Top 20 hits on the Adult Top 40 charts, Mat Kearney appeals to a wide audience with his mix of pop, folk, and hip-hop styles. His lyrics explore relationships and faith and reflect his desire to deepen both. Kearney’s most recent album, January Flower, has a “generous, relaxed vibe,” as one reviewer described. “Kearney frames his warm voice in sparkling acoustic guitars, woody percussion, and burnished synths.” Written between an isolated retreat in Joshua Tree and his home studio, January Flower sees Kearney in his rawest form, distilling the songwriting process and rediscovering the joy of making music.

item.title: Queersgiving - Free Community Meal
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Please join us for Bellefontaine's 1st Annual Queersgiving Community Meal - sponsored by Extending the Branch (non-profit).
Saturday, November 26th, 2022 3:00pm - 5:00pm
Food, Music, Games, and Fellowship provided!
This event is welcome to all with the focus being to offer holiday meals for community members estranged by their families due to their sexual orientation and/or gender identity.
The event will be held at The Olive Tree - 121 W. Chillicothe Ave. in Bellefontaine, OH.
Please visit the link on our Facebook page to register if you can (not required) and/or consider donating to help Extending the Branch further serve the queer community.

item.title: The Grandpa Store & More
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Three years ago, Ellie had a really tough time finding special gifts for her grandparents. After her third trip around the mall, she sighed: “I just wish there was a Grandpa store, where everything there was just for grandpas.” And that is how “The Grandpa Store and More” was born! Join Ellie at The Fun Company for one weekend only and enjoy shopping her curated collection of gifts for grandparents. Cozy slippers, soft blankets, thoughtful photo frames and signs, books, gadgets and a whole lot more! There will be a free gift wrapping station set up for kids to use too! Profits from this pop up store will be used to buy warm coats and winter accessories for kids in need in the Bellefontaine school system. At The Fun Company in Bellefontaine Saturday, December 3rd from 10-8 and Sunday, December 4th from 11-6! Don’t miss it ❤️

item.title: Hometown Parade & City Christmas presented by Citizens Federal & Bellefontaine Grace
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: The Annual First Friday's Christmas Parade has joined with Bellefontaine Grace Church to put on a Christmas event downtown to remember. The evening starts at 4PM with ice skating, a Christmas village, a petting zoo, and wagon rides around the downtown. This year look forward to food trucks around town, traveling carolers singing the spirit of Christmas, and be sure to step into the downtown churches to see them beautifully decorated for the Christmas season. Be sure to find your spot and be ready for when we pause the evening for the Christmas parade at 6PM.

item.title: Brunch with Myles Bowers and the Benjamin Logan HS Jazz Combo

item.title: Hoppy Holidays With Derive Brewing
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Our dear friends from Derive Brewing are back to spend the holidays with us! We will be pouring some rare and festive liquid that they cooked up for us. Ugly Christmas Sweaters are encouraged but not required, and we will be playing a fun white elephant gift exchange game for anyone interested. $20 and under gifts must be here by 7:45 to participate at 8:00.
Derive Tap List: Breyer’s Remorse Imperial Pastry Stout 10.5% Breyers Remorse is a double mashed imperial stout. Brewed with lactose, then conditioned on Mexican, Ugandan, Tahitian, Madagascar and Indonesian vanilla beans. Friends Go Even Further Down South Double Hazy IPA 8.5% Friends Go Even Further Down South: 8.5% DDH Hazy DIPA, brewed with Phantasm, mash hopped and fermented with Omega Cosmic punch to ratchet up the Thiols. Hopped and double dry hopped with our 2022 Nelson Sauvin, Galaxy and Riwaka.
Lost Leatherman Marshmallow English Mild 3.8% The perfect dad beer! This keg was dosed with a healthy portion of marshmallow just for this event!
Friends On Ice Hazy IPA 6.4% The freshest release in our double dry-hopped Friends IPA series brewed and hopped cold-side exclusively with high Lupulin Cryo hops: Citra Incognito, HBC 586, and Sabro.
Eggnog Milk Stout Made for this event. Name and info TBD.

item.title: Til Death Do Us Part Murder Mystery
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join The Syndicate for a murder mystery dinner! All ages are welcomed to enjoy a four course dinner as you flutter through this engaging experience to figure out who did it! The birds are chirping, the sun is shining, but the wedding bells aren’t ringing for this not-so-happy couple when a member of the wedding party is murdered! With a raging bridezilla and an unenthused groom on the scene, it may be happily never after in this nuptial nightmare. Trade clues with your guests and help the hapless couple figure out whodunnit so they can say I do before the wedding hour passes them by! Although it isn't required, costumes are encouraged. The bride and groom aren’t picky, so you can wear just about whatever you’d like. In fact, we encourage you to wear your own wedding dress or the most ridiculous wedding attire you can come up with. It’ll make their special day that much more special, so long as they actually get the chance to walk down the aisle! Get ready for a glamorous evening of mystery, intrigue, and murder at this wedding gone wrong murder mystery party! DINNER MENU Course 1: ﻿Blood "marry" royal red shrimp Course 2: ﻿Smoked Jamaican jerk chicken Course 3: Braised oxtail Course 4: ﻿Deep fried blackberry ice cream over a red velvet waffle We will be serving a couple specialty cocktails this evening as well as our regular drink menu.

item.title: Hawktail
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Hawktail is the instrumental acoustic quartet of fiddler Brittany Haas, bassist Paul Kowert, guitarist Jordan Tice, and mandolinist Dominick Leslie. Flush with orchestral sweeps and sparse vigils, with strains of the American South and the North Atlantic, the band’s cosmopolitan sound is not what you'd expect from a string band.

item.title: Tapestry: A Carole King Tribute
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Starring Jeannie Austin on piano and vocals with her back-up singers and band, Tapestry faithfully recreates the glorious sound of a Carole King concert, leaving you with memories of the great music King wrote and recorded, from “You’ve Got a Friend” to “The Loco-Motion,” and “Natural Woman” to “Pleasant Valley Sunday” and “Beautiful.”

item.title: New Year, New Riff Bourbon and Whiskey Event
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us for a food and bourbon/whiskey pairing evening with New Riff to bring in the New Year! About New Riff We are a new riff on an old tradition, that tradition or theme being Kentucky Bourbon. Founded in 2014 by Ken Lewis, a visionary Kentucky liquor retailer and entrepreneur, New Riff is led by a team of corporate refugees ranging from craft beer to politics and more. New Riff crafts a range of whiskeys—Bourbon, Rye, and eventually Malted Rye and a host of specialty recipes—as well as Kentucky Wild Gin. We are independently owned by one family, and that independence, the freedom from outside pressures and interests has allowed us to make an unforeseen declaration for quality: for perhaps the first time since the inception of the Bottled in Bond Act in 1897, a Kentucky sour mash whiskey distillery has committed itself exclusively to that quality standard, the world’s highest. And we proudly lift that bar even higher by bottling without chill filtration. BOURBON AND FOOD MENU First Course: Kentucky Straight Bourbon Whiskey paired with grilled shrimp flatbread Second Course: Kentucky Straight Rye Whiskey paired with butternut squash carbonara Third Course: Kentucky Single Barrel Rye Whiskey paired with pecan crusted pork cutlet Fourth Course: Kentucky Single Barrel Bourbon Whiskey paired with apple blonde pie General Admission Ticket: General admission will get you in for the bourbon and food pairing. Doors open at 6pm. VIP Experience: This experience starts prior to our tasting and will start at 5:15 and ending at 6:00pm. Doors open at 5pm. After your VIP experience, you will be seated with general admission guests to finish the night with bourbon and food pairing. This events is age 21+ and tickets are limited.

item.title: Downtown Health & Wellness Showcase Weekend

The first 100 registered passports will get an Anytime Fitness/First Fridays swag bag filled with specials and promo items from our downtown businesses. Your bags will be available for pick up at the Chamber office on Thursday 1/5/23 and Friday 1/6/23 from 9am-4pm. Additional Passports will be available at participating locations the weekend of the event.
Check off your passport by visiting the locations and experiencing the self care side of our downtown.

item.dc:creator: admin_sn

item.title: Breabach
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Ranked among Scotland’s most skilled and imaginative contemporary folk acts, Breabach unites deep roots in Highland and Island tradition with the innovative ferment of their Glasgow base. They have released six increasingly acclaimed albums while performing around the world and racking up an impressive array of accolates, including five Scots Trad Music Awards. Come experience the music Songlines has described as, “Exhilarating, energetic, and accomplished”!

item.title: Valentine's Dueling Pianos
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Enjoy Ebony and Ivory Dueling Pianos this Valentine's with sing along songs with a focus on love songs. Come ready with requests as well! We will be serving our event menu that includes small bites and specialty cocktails. This menu will be emailed out closer to the event date to anyone who has purchased a ticket. Seating is general admission and to accommodate all guests, you may be seated with others from another party. If you would like to be seated with others that are attending that you know, please specify in the question during the check out process. Doors open at 6:00pm and the show starts at 6:30pm.

item.title: Darcy Live
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Darcy is a local independent recording artist that brings back the heart and soul of acoustic rock. Drawing from her bluegrass roots, this singer songwriter brings original music as well as all of your favorites from the 90s to now! No band, just Darcy.

item.title: Queen of Hearts Presents the Downtown Bellefontaine Chocolate Walk
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Grab your friends and partake in a sweet stroll through Downtown Bellefontaine. The Chocolate Walk is back and better than ever! Over 20 local businesses will be offering locally sourced sweet treats for you to enjoy! With the purchase of your ticket, you’ll receive over half a pound of chocolate and a passport to help you collect it all. Tickets are only $25 and include an empty chocolate box, over 15 SWEET stops to fill that bad boy up, and a custom shopping bag. Be sure to explore each of your stops to see what great offers they will be hosting.

item.content:encoded:
Date Night Mug Planting Event Bring your Partner, a Pal or Roll Solo
Pick Your Mug Pick Your Plant Put the Two together with a pro

item.title: Sip & Pour
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: You and Your Favorite Person are invited to our February 14th Event at 6pm at the Marketplace (130 S. Main Street) Cafe. Enjoy catering from Brewfountaine and Wine from The Craft Emporium. Michelle from Nana's Creation will teach each participate how to make your own 20 oz candle. Photography of the event will be taken by JamSum Media & Design.

item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us at Brewfontaine for a Trivia Tap Takeover with Sonder Brewing! Prizes include glassware, shirts, and gift cards. Trivia will cover sports, beer, music, and more. No need to come with a team (or do), you can sign up when you get here to have a good time. For future events, visit our Facebook events page. Admission is free for this event.

item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us for a food and bourbon/whiskey pairing evening with Lexington Brewing & Distilling Co. Offerings: Town Branch 90 proof bourbon Town Branch True Cask Town Branch Single Malt Town Branch Rye Food menu to come soon! General Admission Ticket: General admission will get you in for the bourbon and food pairing. Doors open at 6pm. VIP Experience: This experience starts prior to our tasting and will start at 5:15 and ending at 6:00pm. Doors open at 5pm. After your VIP experience, you will be seated with general admission guests to finish the night with bourbon and food pairing. This events is age 21+ and tickets are limited.

item.title: Grand Opening of Rush Creek Art Studio
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: We are very excited to announce the Grand Opening of our new Art Studio and Artist Supply store on January 28th at 10:00 AM. Please come and join our celebration. We will have Fine Art displayed along with some of the best Artist materials from around the world that would please the beginner to the most accomplished artist. We will be offering a beginners art class. If you are interested you can sign up on site or submit a request by e-mail at: rushcreekart@gmail.com

item.title: Galentine
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Girls Night Out!! Our doors will close at 4:30 and reopen at 5:00!! Join us for a shopping experience!! First 20 will receive a swag bag! Enjoy wine and taste special treats from one of our boutique partners, Rose Bud. stay awhile and meet the rest of our boutique partners-they can't wait to meet you!

item.title: Valentine's Day Dinner
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us to celebrate your Valentine February 16-18 during dinner service and receive a complimentary rose. Prime rib dinner menu provided as well as all of our regular offerings. Tables will book quickly so grab yours at syndicatedowntown.com

item.title: Tucker Munz Live
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us to welcome local singer/guitarist, Tucker Munz for his Brewfontaine debut. Covering hits from artist like Tyler Childers & Zach Bryan to classics from Lynyrd Skynyrd & Marshall Tucker Band, with a few originals sprinkled in.

item.title: John Whitehead LIVE
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Born and raised in Bellefontaine, OH, John has been obsessed with music from a young age. From the early days playing in garage bands, to his self-exploration with Apple’s GarageBand, music has always played a formative role in John’s life. His most recent musical direction is the culmination of a life lived through music.

item.title: Annie Jr.
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Local middle schoolers take the stage for this musical based on the popular comic strip and adapted from the Tony Award-winning Best Musical. With equal measures of pluck and positivity, little orphan Annie charms everyone's hearts despite a next-to-nothing start in 1930s New York City. Annie is determined to find the parents who abandoned her years ago at an orphanage run by the cruel Miss Hannigan. Annie eventually foils Miss Hannigan's evil machinations, finding a new home and family in billionaire Oliver Warbucks, his personal secretary, Grace Farrell, and a lovable mutt named Sandy.

item.title: Auditions for Bonnie & Clyde
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Auditions for this Windmill Productions musical are April 8 and 10, with the show scheduled for June 23-25 at the Holland Theatre, 127 E. Columbus Ave, Bellefontaine, Ohio. Auditions will take place on the Holland stage. Please come prepared to sing a song of your choice (doesn't have to be from the musical). Must be at least 16 or older to participate. Sign up for a 20-minute audition time slot here: https://www.signupgenius.com/go/8050a4eaea72aa6f58-bonnie#/ Contact the Holland box office directly with questions.

item.title: Spring Shop Hop Presented by The Logan County Visitors Bureau￼
item.dc:creator: admin_sn

item.content:encoded:
BACK BY POPULAR DEMAND!!
Graze Local is coming back to BUILD for another Charcuterie Board and Wine Paring Class.
This will be an advanced class, however don't let "advanced" discourage you, anyone can participate!
One ticket will allow two people to create and share their charcuterie board at BUILD or you can bring it home. Wine tasting will be paired with the cheeses used that evening. Bring a friend or enjoy date night at BUILD. Everyone is welcome.

item.title: RDBC X Flying Pepper Cantina - Food & Beer Pairing
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Roundhouse Depot Brewing is proud to collaborate once again with the Flying Pepper Cantina to bring to you a unique beer and food pairing experience. Savor delicious and unique Mexican derived dishes paired with our signature beers.

item.title: Live Music with Connor Raines
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us for Live Entertainment featuring Connor Raines

item.title: Lee Rocker
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Lee Rocker made his mark singing, playing, standing on, spinning, and rocking his giant upright bass as a founding member of the Grammy-nominated music group The Stray Cats. Leaders of the rockabilly revival, the Stray Cats sold over 10 million albums, garnered over 23 gold and platinum-certified records, and were music video pioneers. Their “Rock This Town” was named one of the most 500 most important rock songs by the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame. Known for his high energy concerts, Lee Rocker also entertains audiences with behind-the-scenes stories from his forty years in the music business.

item.title: Marc Cohn and Shawn Colvin: Together Onstage
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Two of a generation’s favorite singer-songwriters, Marc Cohn and Shawn Colvin come together onstage for a special evening. Cohn, a Grammy Award winner for his soulful ballad “Walking in Memphis,” and Colvin, winner of the Grammy Award’s Record of the Year and Song of the Year for “Sunny Came Home,” welcome fans into their decades-long friendship for a night of songs and stories.

item.title: An Evening with Tom Rush, Accompanied by Matt Nakoa
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Now celebrating five decades of touring, singer-songwriter Tom Rush has been thrilling audiences here and abroad with hits like No Regrets, Circle Game, Remember Song, Urge for Going and Merrimack County. A profoundly influential voice in the American music scene, Rush helped shape the folk revival in the ’60s, the folk-rock movement of the ‘70s, and then the renaissance of the ’80s and ’90s. Matt Nakoa, a multi-instrumentalist and modern troubador, accompanies and will perform some solo songs throughout the show.

item.title: Sonny Landreth with Cindy Cashdollar
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Sonny Landreth and Cindy Cashdollar are making some rare duo appearances in 2022 and 2023. The Louisiana slide guitar wizard's trademark bottle-neck chops and Cashdollar’s dobro and steel string talents culminate in a showcase of guitar virtuosity. Expect an electric show when these two great instrumental masters perform a mix of original songs and contemporary and traditional blues and roots music. The Suitcase Junket opens.

item.title: Dervish
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Beloved Irish traditional band Dervish has been bringing the sound of their native land to the world for more than 25 years. Described by the BBC as “an icon of Irish music,” the band has played at festivals from Rio to Glastonbury. Dervish’s lineup includes some of Ireland’s finest traditional musicians, fronted by one of the country’s best-known singers, Cathy Jordan. The Guardian newspaper commented: “Dervish are simply brilliant … they carry Irish history with them.” The band is renowned for live performances, which match dazzling sets of tunes with stunning interpretations of traditional songs, and their albums make up one of the outstanding catalogues in Irish music.

item.title: Succulent Workshop
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: We will be planting and learning about succulents! You will get to choose three different succulents. You will be taking the succulents from their nursery pots and planting them into a terra cotta pot! You will leave this event with a nicely potted succulent dish! 💚 I'll be walking you through information about succulents, caring for succulents, light requirements, watering requirements, etc.

item.title: February Plant Swap
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Hello again! If you haven't made it to a plant swap, no worries! Now is the time!! Super laid-back, free event where we bring plant things and leave with new plant things! *I'll be posting info videos and past videos of what people have brought to help you! Everyone who comes is very nice. We would never judge you OR your plant friends!

item.title: Bob Lucas Live
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us to welcome ,local heart throb, Bob Lucas back to Brewfontaine. Bob Lucas is a glory shouting, sweet singing, banjo picking, guitar thumping, old time fiddling, song writing rounder with a desire to share a sense of musical wonder anyway he can. Coming up during the great folk scare of the 60’s he was fortunate to be taken under wing by some of the great traditional musicians and singers. It is the wonder of that era that still holds keen with him.

item.title: 5th Annual Girl Scout Cookie & Beer Pairing
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us for our 5th Annual Girl Scout Cookie & Beer Pairing. One of our most popular events every year! Each ticket includes five different 5oz beers paired with five different Girl Scout cookie varieties (2 cookies per variety). Including a never before released cookie! I﻿n addition to the cookies and beer, ticket holders will get half off of appetizers and $5 pints!! Full boxes of cookies can be purchased at the event as well! This event is general admission seating on the back patio at Brewfontaine. If you would like to be sat with someone in particular, or a certain group, come 10-15 early to have your best shot at preferred seating. Tickets available here: https://www.eventbrite.com/.../5th-annual-girl-scout... This year's Cookie and Beer Pairings: Adventurfuls Indulgent brownie-inspired cookies topped with caramel flavored crème with a hint of sea salt. Paired with: Toppling Goliath Mornin Latte Imperial Stout 8.9% Imperial Coffee Milk Stout brewed with lactose and a careful blend of cacao nibs. Dark chocolate, toasty, sweet coffee taste. Do-si-dos Crunchy oatmeal sandwich cookies with peanut butter filling Paired with: Ellicottville Blackberry Kolsch Fruit Beer 5.2% Brewed with refreshing, mildly dry wild blackberry flavor and aroma. Light, crisp and delicious. Natural, earthy berry sweetness. Samoas Crisp cookies with caramel, coconut, and chocolaty stripes . Paired with: Toxic Hanger 18 Porter 6.2% Classic porter notes of toasted malt, caramel and coffee, balanced by a slight hoppiness and elevated by a last minute addition of coconut for an extra pop of flavor. Thin Mints Crisp, chocolate cookies dipped in a delicious mint chocolaty coating. Paired with: Dragons Milk White White Stout 6% Bourbon Barrel Aged White Stout A full flavored White Stout with strong notes of vanilla, cocoa, coffee roast, and oak from its time in bourbon barrels Raspberry Rally The thin, crispy cookie infused with raspberry flavor and dipped in chocolaty coating*. They may look like Thin Mints’® sister, but these cookies have a delicious taste that’s all their own. They're sure to become a new favorite. Paired with: Unity Vibrations Tart Raspberry Kombucha 8.2% Tart Raspberry is delicious, an otherworldly sour beer with a beautiful color, hints of oak and strong sour raspberry flavor.

item.title: chadwarrencreative Live￼
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: “chadwarrencreative” is an eclectic group of music artists lead by Chad Warren. With a different lineup each performance, you will never hear the same show twice. You can expect influences of soul, classic rock, pop, and hip hop. There is always an intention of connecting with each unique audience. The music is always subject to change and warp in to just the right temperature that equates to “easy listening”. Chad has toured the United States playing shows in places such as Nevada, Arizona, Georgia, Washington D.C., New York, Missouri etc. He has played guitar for multiple country artists from Nashville. Last year he opened for Lee Brice in front of over 10,000 people. Chad guarantees a unique experience and great music. Find original music on Spotify under the name chadwarrencreative.

item.title: March Plant Swap
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Join us at the Café for a FREE plant swap! Check the Facebook Event link for more information!
To participate you must bring ONE of these: plant cutting, plant propagation, whole potted plant, plant accessory, planter pot, or seed packet.

item.title: BootyMix GLOW Pop-Up: Bellefontaine
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
We're bringing one sweat drenching, booty poppin’, soul quenching glow dance party to Bellefontaine, Ohio!
Warning! BootyMix may increase feelings of acceptance of your body and belief in your own ability and skill to conquer your biggest, boldest dreams. Proceed with caution. Not recommended for anyone who wants to play it small and live their life on the sidelines.
Aren't you tired of playing by everyone else's rules? Tired of second guessing what you say or how you're perceived just to make sure you're palatable for the masses? Do you feel numb from watering yourself down so much just to please those around you?
Culture and society have created rules around how we should be… including our sexuality, what we should wear, how we should look, how loud we are, and the roles we should play in our lives. It causes us to judge people based on what we see, causing us to feel judged, so we don’t feel safe showing our authentic selves.
That’s why we create a place where there isn’t a box. No labels. No right or wrong way to move your body or express yourself. A place where you can learn to love yourself just as you are and shed all the labels and bullshit you've been taught to believe. it's time to free yourself and turn the volume up on your life.
Your ticket includes:
Intro to BootyMix: Booty Poppin' Breakdown 1hr GLOW BootyMix Class w/ Amanda Glow accessories! BootyMix Swag available for purchase
What to Expect: Bootymix is a high intensity, hip-hop dance fitness class that gives you the courage to move your body your way and feel comfortable in your own skin.
What to Wear: Neon, bright vibrant colors!
What to Bring: A water bottle and a sweat towel
The After Glow: You’ll leave class with a better sense of self worth and honor for what your body can do. Your mind will be refreshed so you can walk out into the world with a new perspective and confidence to chase those big dreams.

item.title: Easter EGG-Stravaganza Presented by Citizens Federal Savings and Loan
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Hop on over to downtown Bellefontaine as we kick off the spring season with a family friendly event that's sure to please your whole crew! Free crafts & activities will be happening all over downtown all day long, but your paid ticket guarantees you a custom goodie bag and the opportunity to participate in larger activities in the evening. Plus pictures with the Easter Bunny!!! Susie from The Photo Booth will be sending you a digital download of your images after the event. The goody bags are available for just $10 and include a pass for slime egg coloring at The Fun Company, art & yoga at Homegrown Yoga, and cookie decorating with City Sweets & Creamery in the Main St Marketplace. Free activities start in Downtown Bellefontaine at 1:00 PM and your ticketed activates start at 4:00 PM, grab the family and head over to Court St where you will also be able to enjoy food trucks, a live petting zoo from Marmon Valley Farms, and some sidewalk chalk fun! View the full schedule events at www.firstfridaysbellefontaine.com!

item.title: Trivia Tap Takeover w/ Outerbelt Brewing
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us at Brewfontaine for a Trivia Tap Takeover with Outerbelt Brewing! Prizes include glassware, shirts, and gift cards. Trivia will cover sports, beer, music, and more. No need to come with a team (or do), you can sign up when you get here to have a good time. For future events, visit our Facebook events page. Admission is free for this event

item.title: Basket Making Workshop
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Basket Weaving Workshop!! 🧺 In this workshop you will weave a 5'' x 5'' Basket with a 12'' Handle. Date: Sunday, April 2nd Time: 12:00pm-3:00pm (3 hour class) Cost: $55 per person Location: The Poppy Seed Snacks & Wine will be provided. Tickets are NOW live so be sure to get yours. Only 8 reserved spots.

item.title: St. Patrick's Day Celebration
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Come Celebrate St.Patricks Day. Opening at 3pm Tapping our Irish Rail Red Ale. Schmidt's Sausage Food Truck serving delicious eats 5 to 8 Wear your green attire Bring your friends drink a pint with us.

item.title: Columbus Jazz Quartet
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Columbus Jazz Quartet brings the exciting sounds of classic and modern jazz to the Holland! This collection of talented Central Ohio musicians including Derek DiCenzo (bass), Sydney McSweeney (vocals), Lucas Holmes (piano), and Logan County native Isaac Bednarki (percussion). The quartet performs all styles of jazz including bebop, blues, Latin, ballads, and more. Tickets start at just $10 for adults and $5 for youth!

item.title: Sliders & Suds￼
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Join us for our first-ever beer dinner, Sliders and Suds! This will be a 5 course food and beer pairing with a focus on different sliders. We look forward to hosting you!

BEER: Masthead Hazy Headlines IPA
NEIPA 6.5% Cleveland, OH
Hazy Headlines is brewed with our favorite hand selected hops to elicit notes of pineapple, passion fruit, mango, and orange zest. Expect to be greeted by a densely hazy pour., incredible hop flavor, virtually no bitterness and a luscious finish.
PAIRING: Firecracker Shrimp (shrimp deep fried and tossed in sweet Thai chili sauce served atop avocado mousse and garnished with tuxedo sesame seeds)

BEER: R. Shea Nut Brown Ale (TBD)
Brown Ale 4.75% Akron, OH
This ale is a thirst-quenching, easy drinker after a hard day's work downtown. Subtle nuttiness from the darker malts lends to its signature flavor and clean finish.
PAIRING: Slider, baby portobello cap stuffed with caramelized onions, garlic braised mushrooms covered with swiss cheese and a brown ale gravy

BEER:Masthead Jalapeno IPA
IPA 6.8% Cleveland, OH
Brewed with fresh jalapenos, the fruity notes from hand-selected hops pair well with that unmistakable aroma and flavor, all backed up with just the right level of spiciness.
PAIRING: Slider, burger patty topped with pepper jack cheese, shishito salsa and a toasted cumin and lime crema

BEER: Toxic Okay Karen Pilsner
Pilsner 5% Dayton, OH
Made in collaboration with our friends at Coco's Bistro, a Dayton favorite in fine dining since 2006. A delicate malty sweetness is balanced out by a pleasant hop bitterness in this straw colored, effervescent classic style pilsner.
PAIRING: Slider, crispy pork belly topped with smoked tomato compote and a creamy brussel sprout slaw

BEER: R Shea Our Saving Grace Flutternutter Stout
Double Pastry Stout 11.25% Akron, OH
Peanut butter and marshmallow combine forces to fight an invisible, apocalyptical villain.
PAIRING: Chocolate coffee sponge cake with a hazelnut sweet cream icing and garnished with shaved chocolate bits

This event is age 21+. Please answer the questions in checkout if you have any dietary restrictions or if there is another party that you would like to be seated with.

The doors will open at 5:30pm for seating and the pairings will begin at 6pm.

item.title: Sip n Paint
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us for another great evening with friends and painting a wonderful spring piece. Enjoy a pint while you paint. Tickets available

item.title: B-Town BrewFest
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: B-Town Brew Fest is back and we’re brewing up some fun in Downtown Bellefontaine this May! This event is brought to you by our friends over at Alan Galvez Insurance & Royer Realty. THE DEETS: Mark your calendar for May 5th from 5-8pm. You will get10 tasting tickets at check-in to use throughout the downtown to sample a variety of Ohio made beer, and even an exclusive brew from our friends at Roundhouse Depot Brewing. Four Acre Clothing will be on-site screen printing special edition shirts. Downtown will be sprinkled with food trucks and plenty of activities to participate in along your tasting route. Human Billiards, Kick Darts and who wants to ride a mechanical bull? GENERAL ADMISSION TICKETS: Purchase of a general admission ticket will land you 10 tasting tickets, each redeemable for 5 ounces of beer at designated stops, a commemorative collapsible cooler filled with snacks, and a tasting cup. DESIGNATED DRIVER TICKETS: All the merch perks of general admission without the extra cost of alcohol plus a NA beverage on us! MISC INFORMATION: Check-in will be 4:30-6:30pm at the Logan County Chamber. All Bellefontaine First Friday events are brought to you by our friends at Mary Rutan Heath. All ticket sales are final. Must be 21 or older to participate. This event will be held rain or shine. ID MUST BE PRESENTED AT CHECK-IN. WITHOUT THE PRESENCE OF YOUR ID (regardless of age and who you might know that's a volunteer), ACCESS WILL BE DENIED TO THIS EVENT.

item.title: Mounted Plant Workshop @ The Poppy Seed Boutique
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us at The Poppy Seed Boutique in Bellefontaine, Ohio. We are so excited to partner up with them at their beautiful location for this workshop! Enjoy light refreshments & great company! Grab your pals for an afternoon out or come solo! You will have a great time either way! Cost: $65 per guest Only 12 spots available

item.title: Charcuterie Class
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us at the Roundhouse for an evening of friends, food, and learning something new. Learn how putting cheese and charcuterie staples together creates a fantastic board perfect for entertaining! Bring a friend, have a drink and learn a new skill.

item.title: Downtown Bellefontaine Spring Cleaning 2023
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: The Downtown Bellefontaine Spring Cleaning is back! Join the Downtown Bellefontaine community to celebrate Earth Day and clean up our downtown before we head into spring and summer. Volunteers will get a free lunch from Six Hundred Downtown, a t-shirt, and one complementary refreshment from Brewfontaine after the event! Sign up by Friday, April 14 to be guaranteed a t-shirt. Check in at 9:30 am at the Main Street Marketplace. Sign up now at: https://downtownbellefontaine.com/cleanup/

item.title: HattoriHanzo Shears Haircutting class w/ Joel Hiatt
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Texture cutting class presented by HattoriHanzo Shears • Refreshments & lunch provided by Undertone salons • Must be a licensed cosmetologist • Seating is limited so please confirm your spot by clicking “going”

item.title: A Tribute to the Mills Brothers
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Celebrate the music of The Mills Brothers! Members of this influential jazz and pop group of the 1930s-'50s spent much of their childhood in Bellefontaine. The Logan County Youth Jazz Orchestra under the direction of Myles Bowers with guest conductor and arranger Mike Kamuf will premiere Kamuf's Mills Brothers Medley arrangement. Presented by LoCo Art and the Holland, this event also celebrates the Mills Brothers mural created by local students, with artist Duarte Brown as a special guest. Downtown Bellefontaine will be hosting Pineapple-Palooza from 4 to 11, so plan to enjoy food trucks, a zipline, and other entertainment before and after the concert! Parking may be limited closer to showtime.

item.title: Light Up the Holland With Contagious Comedy
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: You’ll want to catch it! Join Contagious Comedy stars Steven Scott and Nicholle Kun with radio personality Dino Tripodis as master of ceremonies for this evening of laughter. Proceeds benefit the Light Up the Holland marquee campaign. George Kun, Contagious Comedy’s COO, grew up in Bellefontaine after escaping Iron Curtain-era Hungary. He and his daughter Nicholle, a successful New York City comedian who founded Contagious Comedy, share a passion for giving back to good causes and communities. Eight tables in front of the stage are also available for purchase; contact the Holland box office for more information.

item.title: E5C4P3 -- The Journey Tribute
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Kick off your Fourth of July weekend with Journey! For thirty years, E5C4P3 has expertly re-created the music of one of rock history's most beloved bands. Recognized as the original and best Journey tribute band ever to exist, E5C4P3 values note-for-note accuracy and performs each show with the high-energy excitement of a real Journey concert.
Focused on the Steve Perry era, E5C4P3 visually re-creates the essence of early ‘80s Journey. Expect all the hits from “Lights” and “Wheel In The Sky” to “Separate Ways” and “Be Good To Yourself,” as well as occasional surprises for all the die-hard Journey fans!

item.title: April Plant Swap
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
April plant swap at Main Street Marketplace Cafe! All you HAVE to bring is ONE of these: propagation, plant cutting (remember the node), planter pot, plant accessory, seed packet, or potted plant. We start at 12p, that will be the best time to snag the best plants.
You can ALWAYS bring your DORA cup! https://www.ci.bellefontaine.oh.us/dora.html *If you're nervous, bring a pot or an accessory. Then you can participate without committing to plants. See less See less

item.title: Dogfish Head Record Store Day 2023
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Come turn up the volume with Dogfish Head on Record Store Day 2023. We'll be spinning records starting at 11 AM and pouring some amazing beers from Dogfish Head . Complimentary DFH pint glasses and posters while supplies last, as well as other swag, starting at 11 AM.

item.title: Mugging for Mom
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Plant a mug for your mom, with your mom or just because... Need an easy gift for Mother's Day? Create a fun potted coffee mug for your mama with the pros from unboxed boutique and Beck Plant Haus! Here's how it works: 1. Show up for this event. 2. Purchase snarky, sweet or somewhere in between mug from unboxed boutique's large variety. 3. Purchase an adorable plant from the plant gurus as Beck Plant Haus. 4. Let the pros help you pot that cutie in your new mug. 5. Bask in the glow of your new plant in all its glory. Earn extra brownie points and invite your mom on a date to come plant her mug WITH you. Don't have a mom or a mom relationship? Get free mom hugs from us! (We're chill like that.) What makes this event a true win? You can sip on your favorite beverages from Roundhouse Depot Brewing Company while you "work". Win-win, right?

item.title: Cinco De Mayo
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us for an Epic Celebration. Beer Margaritas Mexican Lager Taste of Paradise Cider Taco Bout Food Truck 5 to 8 serving up authentic Mexican food

item.title: Pineapple Palooza presented by Four Acre Clothing Co
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: The second annual Pineapple Palooza presented by Four Acre Clothing Co is a night not to miss in Downtown Bellefontaine. Last year was a huge hit and we plan to make it even bigger this year! Zip Line down Main Street, try out your surfing skills, race down Court St in a Zorb ball with your friends, test your core balance at the log rolling challenge! There's more for the kids too with a pirate bounce house, inflatable obstacle course, giant tropical slide and an inflatable wrecking ball game, and 4 way basketball. Face painting and balloon artist will be on hand, along with Dunk a Cop! We've got dinner covered with 7 different food trucks, lemon shake ups, and Hall's Kettle Corn. Vendors will be lining Columbus Ave. with their goods and information. At 8:00 pm Cherry Blonde will take the stage for a rocking good time and to carry us through the rest of the night. The whole night starts at 4:00 PM with the attractions and activities closing up at 9:00 PM, and the band topping off the night until 11:00 PM. Downtown will host a beer garden to help make those beverage lines a little shorter and easier to get through. Bring the family down for a fun filled evening. Event wristbands will be valid for all attractions except for the Zipline. Tickets for the Zipline can be purchased at the Zipline for $15 ea.

item.title: Cinco De Mayo Menu Takeover
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Mark your calendars for one of our most popular events of the year!
APPETIZERS Trio Dip Plate : House fried tortilla chips, served with black bean dip, elote, and queso Loaded Nacho : House fried tortilla chips, topped with queso, shredded lettuce, shredded cheddar cheese, and pico de gallo, add shredded chicken, pulled pork, or ground beef. ENTRÉES Taco Plate: Three flour tortillas, with your choice of shredded chicken, pulled pork, or ground beef, topped with shredded lettuce, shredded cheddar cheese, and pico de gallo. Served with refried beans and Spanish rice. Wet Brewritto: Your choice of shredded chicken, pulled pork, or ground beef in a flour tortilla, refried beans, Spanish rice, shredded lettuce, shredded cheddar cheese, and pico de gallo. Topped with queso and green onion. Quesadilla: Pepper jack and cheddar cheese, served with shredded lettuce, sliced tomato, sour cream, and salsa. shredded chicken, pulled pork, or ground beef. SOUP Chicken tortilla soup: Shredded chicken, tomato, black bean, corn, onion, garlic, jalapeno, green bell peppers, in a house made chicken stock. Topped with shredded cheddar cheese, and fried tortilla chips. SALAD Taco Salad: Spring mix, ground beef, shredded cheddar cheese, pico de gallo, fried tortilla strips, served with Guacamole, salsa, and sour cream. DESSERT Churro: Traditional churro, fried in house, tossed in cinnamon sugar, topped with chocolate and caramel drizzle.
