item.title: Chad Warren Creative LIVE￼
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Join us for our 2023 live music series, every Saturday from 7-10pm! Admission is free and is first come first served in our Beer Garden.

Are you someone that can't imagine living without music?
Is it essential to who you are and how you live your life?
Has music been there for you in a way no one has?
If you are anything like me I'm guessing your answer might be yes.
I write/ play/ listen to music because I need it. It's like the air I breathe. Can you relate?
The music I make has my fingerprint on every single aspect from writing, mixing and mastering to design and marketing. Don't get me wrong I do not do this alone. I have an amazing team that I am forever grateful for, but I do not take shortcuts, I take extra care to insure the integrity of my work.
​If you're like me don't settle, find what makes you tick don't stop. Ever.
w﻿ww.chadwarrencreative.com

item.title: Downtown Bellefontaine Art & Wine Walk Presented by Six Hundred Downtown
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: The Art & Wine Walk in Downtown Bellefontaine presented by Six Hundred Downtown is definitely something to look forward to. With 26 stops featuring artists and wine, food trucks, live art demonstrations, and entertainment, it's sure to be a night to remember. And the fact that your ticket allows you to sample 10 Ohio wines with the first 50 tickets sold getting a complementary Charcuterie cup is a great bonus. Get the traveling paint and sip add-on ticket to add an activity to your tour. Fill in the colors for your own Downtown Art and Wine canvas bag with acrylic paint markers provided at your wine stops. Limited tickets available for this fun activity so don't miss out. If you're interested in attending, be sure to click the link for all the details and pricing.

item.title: Crawfish Boil
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: We are very excited to partner with Missing Mountain Brewing for our 2023 Crawfish boil! Missing Mountain Brewing Company is a Cuyahoga Falls brewery sitting on the banks of the Cuyahoga River. Committed to providing the local community and visitors an atmosphere to celebrate and enjoy the beauty of Cuyahoga Falls with great beer and great food. Founded by award winning home brewers, Dave Bertolotti, Keith McFarlane, Danny Yagersz and Dan Kephart. The name, Missing Mountain, is to reflect their love for winter sports and the outdoors. As Northeast Ohioans, they love the region and all its fantastic outdoor opportunities. There are rivers, valleys, a National Park, great regional and local parks, and Lake Erie. As avid snowboarders and skiers, They felt the only thing that is missing is a mountain. Our Missing Mountain draft line up for this year’s event will be: Zwickle Trickle | Pale Ale 5.6% Our take on a Hazy/New England style pale ale. This beer is hopped exclusively with Mosaic hops. Prince Juice | Fruit Beer 5.3% She wore a raspberry beret! The artist named Prince or (Formally Known As Prince) inspired the name of this fruited ale brewed with lactose and conditioned on raspberry puree and vanilla beans. Think Raspberry smoothie. Not too sweet, not too tart. Somewhere in the middle! Enjoy! Beach Loungin’ | Lager 4.5% Beach Loungin' is a refreshingly crisp, light, and flavorful lager. This thirst-quenching lager can be enjoyed anytime, whether hanging with friends, mowing the lawn, or just lounging at the beach! Enjoy the day; you deserve it! The all-you-can-eat menu will consist of: -Crawfish Boil - Fresh Louisiana Crawfish (delivered LIVE the day of the event) -Andouille Sausage -Shrimp -Corn on the Cob -Onion and Red Potatoes -Collard Greens w/ Pancetta -Jalapeño Cheddar Corn Bread w/ Old Bay Compound Butter. All tickets are general admission and include the menu above, a Missing Mountain logo beer glass, live music from Max Razinger, and your first beer. Not to mention access to private party in the Syndicate beer garden! 7Thirty8 Apparel will be on site live printing event tee shirts from their mobile print truck. This event is 21+ If you would like to be seated with a particular party outside of your tickets, please specify the name(s) of the individuals in the custom question area during checkout. We will do our best to accommodate all requests. Tables are of 6 seats. *Should the event need to be moved due to weather, the event will be moved to the following day, Aug 2, and will be announced 24 hours in advance. For future events, please visit syndicatedowntown.com

item.title: Jared Foster LIVE
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us for our 2023 live music series, every Saturday from 7-10pm! Admission is free and is first come first served in our Beer Garden. Jared Foster is a singer, songwriter and performer from Lancaster, Ohio. Jared completed his degree in Commercial Music Production and a Minor in Business at University of Cincinnati's College Conservatory of Music. As a solo artist and hired gun, Jared has performed in cities all over Ohio, including gigs in Kentucky and Indiana. While performing, Jared plays all genres of music, but prefers acoustic soft rock music. When it comes to musical instruments, he mainly plays acoustic and electric guitar, but also dabbles in bass guitar, drums, piano, mandolin, and the occasional ukulele. When Jared isn’t writing music or performing, he can be found enjoying a grilled steak, following the Ohio State Buckeyes, or enjoying the great outdoors. w﻿ww.jaredfostermusic.com

Since then he has nurtured his sound blending pop, alt-country, folk and soul. Lyrical verses, a soulful vocal presence and finesse guitar playing styles are all crucial ingredients to JT’s music, inspired by the works of contemporary recording artists & singer-songwriters like John Mayer, Michael Jackson, Ryan Adams, Ed Sheeran and Amos Lee to name a few. w﻿ww.jthilliermusic.com

item.title: Bob Lucas LIVE
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us for our 2023 live music series, every Saturday from 7-10pm! Admission is free and is first come first served in our Beer Garden. Bob Lucas is a glory shouting, sweet singing, banjo picking, guitar thumping, old time fiddling, song writing rounder with a desire to share a sense of musical wonder anyway he can. Coming up during the great folk scare of the 60’s he was fortunate to be taken under wing by some of the great traditional musicians and singers. It is the wonder of that era that still holds keen with him. As times turned hip in the late 60’s he too became a part of that culture. The Dancer Inside of You, Bob's 1972 release, received a 4 star rating from Downbeat Magazine. Two songs off Dancer were picked up and recorded by the legendary Newgrass Revival, who went on to recorded many of his songs in years that followed. In 1977, Bob co-founded the culture-morphing band Eclectricity, one of the first bands to bring 'gypsy' and 'klezmer' music to a North American folk-music audience. Eclectricity found a fan in, and often toured with, actor/singer Theodore Bikel. It was at this time Bob learned that the word “macaronic” has nothing to do with pasta. In 1986 Bob began working for Mad River Theater Works writing songs and making plays for what has become an ever-growing audience. Songs from this period of Bob's writing were recorded by the blissfully talented Alison Krauss on her 1996 Grammy Award winning album, So Long So Wrong, and again on her 2001 release, New Favorite. In addition to the many recordings he has made with Mad River Theater Works in the past two decades he has recorded three albums of original songs, Rushsylvania, Kin, and most recently, Banjo For Lovers. Having grown up in a family of singers and musicians it is only natural that Bob would continue to play music with his family. With Chloe Manor his daughter and her husband Chris Westhoff they make up the Hedgehog String Band. This ensemble makes music with power, thrust and charm that is just down right good and dancy. These three do not draw the line at old time traditional music. As has been so often the case, tradition is a springboard that gives the lift and velocity to dive into the ever-welcoming pool of twenty first century contemporary music. Off they go without a splash. w﻿ww.boblucasmusic.com

item.title: Moonshine & Wine LIVE
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us for our 2023 live music series, every Saturday from 7-10pm! Admission is free and is first come first served in our Beer Garden. New Beginnings: James & Danielle’s modern creation of an original inception with a different onset and a unique preface that concludes their past and rids the familiar end that can be antiquated and terminates the usual result of negativity and replacing it with absolute positive aura. Their songs wound through generations of genres such as bluegrass, folk, country, and bits of rock, while still staying on course with their own vision. James and Danielle's harmonies can have you mesmerized all the while clinging onto every word filled with emotion, enthusiasm, and passion.

item.title: Topher James LIVE
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us for our 2023 live music series, every Saturday from 7-10pm! Admission is free and is first come first served in our Beer Garden. Refusing to take the route of his peers Topher James shunned the corporate world after a pivotal moment with his boss and began a journey of music, for a living, as a way of life. A common denominator of all the music he loved growing up was melody, strong emotional content, and soul. Studying the likes of classic artists such as Sam Cooke and Donny Hathaway as well as modern R&B and Gospel artists like PJ Morton and Emily King Topher quickly put together a band. Forming in 2014 Topher James and Biscuit Brigade has grown and evolved into a powerhouse soul band featuring rich vocal harmony and funky guitar riffs pleasing crowds everywhere. The bands first EP “Art & Soul” recorded in southern Ohio with Josh Antonuccio at the helm came out to great reviews: GroundSounds.com said it was a “…stellar 6-track EP rich with funky guitar licks, smooth horn section melodies, and lyrically rich storytelling…” IAmTunedUp.com called it ”…a straightforward classy album. It accurately reflects Topher James’ personality - confident, opinionated and carrying an affinity for the finer things in life…The image of this new project is not pretentious - and neither is the music.“ w﻿ww.topherjamesmusic.com

item.title: Andy Shaw LIVE
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us for our 2023 live music series, every Saturday from 7-10pm! Admission is free and is first come first served in our Beer Garden. The Andy Shaw Band is a reggae infused rock band with a strong blend of vocal harmonies, and family roots. The band's core is comprised of Andy Shaw (guitar/vocals), Chris Shaw (drums/vocals), Jim Shaw (keyboard/vocals). Performing since 2005, the band has evolved into integrating more roots reggae and fuller harmonies. Utilizing Chris Shaw’s uniquely crafted reggae drum skills, and Andy’s flair for great melody writing and vocal ability, the band crafts a sound that makes you dance, AND feel something in your soul. The band has done numerous tours to Texas, NYC, Nashville, Florida, and all points in between. They’ve performed at Hookahville Festival, the Werkout Music Festival, Musikfest 2013, Red Gorilla Showcase at SXSW, Midpoint Music Festival, and have risen to one of the regions PREMIER original acts. Andy Shaw Band has a reputation as one of the hardest working acts around, and they are spreading the music beyond their home town of Columbus, Ohio. As they take the next step forward, the future is bright, and the music is the perfect soundtrack to take them there. w﻿ww.andyshawband.com

item.title: The Hooten Hallers LIVE
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us for our 2023 live music series, every Saturday from 7-10pm! Admission is free and is first come first served in our Beer Garden. For the past sixteen years, The Hooten Hallers have been crisscrossing the country as inveterate road warriors, bringing their peculiar vision of Americana: a fiery, bluesy, rock and roll fever dream birthed in Missouri’s fertile musical heartland. They’ve put so many miles into the road that they’ve burned through multiple tour vans and left twisted metal and frayed rubber strewn across the road behind them. The Hooten Hallers are known for their incendiary live shows that must be experienced to be believed, taking the listener on a dynamic journey from explosively raucous blues to expressive three-part vocal harmonies to danceable grooves. The enduring hope and tinges of madness from this power trio are driven by the infernal vocal growl and swirling electric and lap steel guitars of John Randall, the powerful drumming and falsetto howl of Andy Rehm, and the burning baritone and bass saxophone lines of Kellie Everett. The Hooten Hallers have always been musical colliders, smashing together blues, garage rock, country, punk, and a hint of jazz into a refreshingly unique sound. It’s Morphine meets ZZ Top mixed with a dash of George Thorogood and Tom Waits. But anyone who has seen this band live knows that this group is unlike anything they’ve experienced before. When The Hooten Hallers come to town, you know it’s gonna be a party! w﻿ww.thehootenhallers.com

item.title: An Evening with Roger McGuinn
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Turn! Turn! Turn! As front man of influential ‘60s group The Byrds, Roger McGuinn was on the leading edge of that era, with hits from “Eight Miles High” to “Mr. Tambourine Man.” McGuinn later went on to a solo career, exploring his folk roots and rock sensibilities and collaborating with artists including Tom Petty and Elvis Costello. McGuinn was inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame for his work with the Byrds. This concert will be a rich reminder of this musical legend’s many contributions!

item.title: Church Basement Ladies
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join Windmill Productions for some heavenly humor! Church Basement Ladies celebrates the church basement kitchen and the women who work there. This musical comedy features four women and their relationships -- plus their beloved pastor -- as they organize the food and the problems of a rural Minnesota church. From the elderly matriarch of the kitchen to the young bride-to-be, these down-to-earth ladies handle a record-breaking Christmas dinner, the funeral of a dear friend, a Hawaiian Easter Fundraiser, and a steaming hot July wedding with heart and humor. Shows will be at 7:30 p.m. Saturday, Aug. 5, and 2 p.m. Sunday, Aug. 6.

item.title: Cup O'Joe
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
A folk and bluegrass band based in Northern Ireland, Cup O’ Joe features the tight sibling harmonies of Tabitha, Benjamin, and Reuben Agnew, plus Tabitha’s husband David Benedict. The band’s unique mix of original compositions and re-imagined traditional songs paired with exciting live performances have led to growing acclaim in the acoustic music scene in Europe and beyond.

Expect a delightful evening of music with these brilliant young performers!

item.title: Pauly Shore: Stick With the Dancing
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Comedian Pauly Shore brings his new autobiographical show to the Holland! In this hilarious, intimate evening, he’ll revisit his childhood at the Comedy Store, career ups and downs, and the roller coaster of Beverly Hills High School, MTV days, opening for Sam Kinison, and much more.

Revisit the ‘90s and more during this laughter-filled trip down memory lane with the Weasel himself!

item.title: WCOC Concert Band: "Tale of the Tape"
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
West Central Ohio Community Concert Band presents "Tale of the Tape," featuring music from your favorite film scores! The band is excited to offer selections from composers including John Williams, Danny Elfman, and Hans Zimmer.

Directed by Myles Bowers, the all-volunteer band includes amateur and professional musicians, educators, and students hailing from multiple Ohio counties. Join them at a night for the whole family to enjoy as you relive some of cinema's greatest soundtrack achievements!

Admission is FREE, but a ticket is required to make sure everyone has a seat at this popular annual event.

item.title: Patrick Sweany Live
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Join us for our 2023 live music series, every Saturday from 7-10pm! Admission is free and is first come first served in our Beer Garden.

Patrick Sweany lives for the Grooves.
Whether it's the ones he records on his albums, the ones he lays down with his band on the stage, or the ones that define his days writing songs and living life in his E. Nashville neighborhood, the Grooves are what have propelled Sweany through 20+ years of performing and recording across 8 studio albums and dozens of countries around the world. Being in the Groove is Sweany's recipe for success.
To say 2020 has disrupted these Grooves, is a severe understatement. The cancellation of live music in the U.S. for the past several months initially ground Sweany's Groove to a halt. However, it hasn't all been bad. The time away from the road and away from the studio has forced some introspection and reflection.
Serendipitously, Sweany had begun a new experiment in 2019 teaming up with Andrew Trube and Anthony Farrell (the Austin duo known as Greyhounds) to rework and re-record some of his oldest compositions. Taking songs from his first two records, Sweany headed into Bud's recording Services, Greyhounds' studio while on tour in Texas.
Together with his touring band, Sweany reworked about a dozen older songs, and even tracked a cover and a never-released tune. The experience was rewarding and fun, but with tour dates pending, the rough tracks sat on the shelf for several more months. Once back in Nashville, Sweany began working with local producer Dexter Green at 3 Sirens Studio. Dex began meticulously shaping and mixing them, and Get That Feeling was born.
Nine Mile Records is releasing two batches of these recordings (volumes I & II) in 2020 and 2021 while Sweany writes and records another full-length album of new compositions.
The songs represent both a look back over Sweany's long career, and new collaborations that showcase some sides of Patrick's songs you might find surprising.
​It's never too late to find a new Groove.
w﻿ww.patricksweany.com

item.title: John Whitehead Live
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Born and raised in Bellefontaine, OH, John has been obsessed with music from a young age. From the early days playing in garage bands, to his self-exploration with Apple’s GarageBand, music has always played a formative role in John’s life. His most recent musical direction is the culmination of a life lived through music.

item.title: Loft & Hidden Spaces Tour of Downtown Bellefontaine
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Join your friends and neighbors on a fun historic walking tour of several historic buildings in Downtown Bellefontaine. Explore upper floor lofts and hidden spaces on Saturday, August 26th from 1:00 – 4:00 pm.

This self-guided tour will also include several newly redeveloped and completed upper floor spaces with breath-taking finishes, living and working spaces, and forgotten about hidden spaces throughout the downtown.

Purchase your $10 tickets in advance. Check in will take place inside the Main Street Marketplace. Limited tickets will be available at check in.

All proceeds raised will benefit the Downtown Bellefontaine Partnership.

item.title: Downtown Bellefontaine Back to School Bash, presented by Prescription Bliss
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Can you believe it's almost time to head back to school? The summer has flown by, but let's bid it farewell with a fantastic bash! Join us downtown on September 1st from 4pm-7pm for an unforgettable end-of-summer party that promises fun for the whole family.
We have an exciting lineup of activities planned, including a DJ dance party, cake walk, a time capsule activity and yard games. You'll also find various vendors offering their wares, and we'll be serving free hot dogs and popcorn to all attendees. Stay tuned for the announcement of scheduled activities and games! Be sure to also grab your shopping passport at the check in table to find all of she shopping deals downtown during the event. Passports that are turned back in will go into a drawing to win one of three shopping spree checks!
To make your evening even more enjoyable, consider purchasing a $15 kids passport. With the kids passport, you'll have access to face painting, a special craft session with LoCo Art, and a fun photo opportunity at the photobooth. Best of all, every dollar from passport sales will go towards purchasing teacher supplies to help them fill up their classrooms. If you'd like to contribute by dropping off a donation at one of our participating locations, you'll receive a $5 discount on your passport! Click here to purchase your passport now, and view the list of suggested donations and drop off locations.
All the excitement will take place at the big parking lot behind the Main Street Market Place, where the event check-in will also be a donation drop and passport pick up. We can't wait to see you there!

item.title: Macramé Plant Hanger Workshop
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Join us for a beginner friendly class where you’ll learn the art of macramé. Create your own stunning plant hanger to take home with you. Your instructor Jess will guide you through some of the basic macramé knots and how to put them together to make something beautiful. In such a fast-paced world, macramé is a wonderful way to slow down and truly be present in your craft.

The Details:

Mark your calendars for Thursday, August 24th from 6-8 PM. This event will be held at The Photo Booth Studio at 137 South Main St, Bellefontaine, OH, 43311. All materials will be provided.

Tickets:

$35 per guest and you get to take home your finished plant hanger. No refunds for no shows. In the event you must cancel your reservation, if its 48-hour notice, you may request a refund via email triplemoonproducts@gmail.com. No-shows and cancellations made the day before or same day as class will not be refunded.

item.title: Remembering Red – A Tribute to Red Skelton
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
A Tribute to Red Skelton is a heartfelt trip down memory lane. Reminisce as Brian Hoffman performs the jokes and routines that made Red Skelton famous. Brian’s expert portrayal of Mr. Skelton will have you in stitches as he re-creates some of Red’s most well known characters. Laugh with Clem Kadiddlehopper. Giggle along with Gertrude and Heathcliffe, the two seagulls. Feel the love of Freddy the Freeloader, the hobo who lived in the city dump.

Generously sponsored by Belser Accounting and Tax Service

Season Sponsors: Beasley Architecture & Design and Marker, Inc.; Media Sponsors: Bellefontaine Examiner and V-Teck Communications; Season Support: Honda of America and Ohio Arts Council

item.title: Bob Lucas Live
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: We are blessed to have the musical talent of Bob Lucas! Bob is a glory shouting, sweet singing, banjo picking, guitar thumping, old time fiddling, song writing rounder with a desire to share a sense of musical wonder anyway he can.

item.title: Ken Waldman & The Wild Ones Ft. Dom Flemons
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
It's an interdisciplinary arts extravaganza with a troupe of eight musicians: a poet, a dancer, a founding member of the famed Carolina Chocolate Drops, a banjo virtuoso, and more!

Grammy-winner Dom Flemons has gone from being a founding member of the Carolina Chocolate Drops to an acclaimed international solo career. Longtime Alaska resident Ken Waldman is a writer with twenty books and a fiddler with twelve CDs. The lineup also includes virtuoso banjo player and fiddler Aaron Jonah Lewis; Hannah Lewis, multi-instrumentalist and prize-winning percussive dancer; and guitarist Chris Westhoff. Expect fun, inspiration, and surprises!

Generously sponsored by 91.3 WYSO Public Radio

Season Sponsors: Beasley Architecture & Design and Marker, Inc.; Media Sponsors: Bellefontaine Examiner and V-Teck Communications; Season Support: Honda of America and Ohio Arts Council

item.title: The Great Pumpkin Walk of Downtown Bellefontaine, presented by Small Nation
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Join us for the highly anticipated Giant Pumpkin Walk of Downtown Bellefontaine, Presented by Small Nation! This event is a must-attend in downtown Bellefontaine, and we guarantee that this year's edition will exceed all expectations.
Not only will you have the opportunity to enjoy a wide range of free activities downtown, but we have also added some exciting new attractions that you won't want to miss. For just $20, you can purchase a kids passport that grants you access to these amazing experiences!
This years free activities will include a petting zoo and trick or treating and craft activities at retail locations, a free shopping passport with discounts and free items from our downtown retailers and possibly a straw maze!
With the purchase of the $20 passport, your child will have unlimited bounces on the pumpkin bounce house, tiger combo and castle. Additionally, they will have the chance to participate in a mini photo shoot at The Photo Booth. As if that wasn't enough, they will get a stroll through downtown on a hayride and each child will also receive a FREE trip through the caramel apple bar to make their own delicious caramel apple creation!
Please note that each child will require their own passport for these added activities. To collect your special VIP wristband, please check in in front of the courthouse. Individual bounce house tickets will just be $1 per trip and can be purchased at check-in.
If you have any questions or need further information, feel free to reach out to Micky Groves, our Downtown Director, at (937) 599-5121. We are eagerly looking forward to seeing you in Downtown Bellefontaine!

item.title: Lightning Express - Everly Brothers Tribute
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
The magical harmonies and classic hits of Don and Phil Everly, the Everly Brothers, come to life again at every Lightning Express show. Influencing artists from the Beatles to Tom Petty, The Everly Brothers showed the way with their singing, songwriting, and style. Lightning Express recreates the spirit, energy, and feeling of a live Everly Brothers show.

Generously sponsored by Jason and Ann Vogel

Season Sponsors: Beasley Architecture & Design and Marker, Inc.; Media Sponsors: Bellefontaine Examiner and V-Teck Communications; Season Support: Honda of America and Ohio Arts Council

item.title: Invasion of the Body Snatchers
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Get your Halloween thrills and chills at this FREE movie! Dr. Miles Bennell returns to his small town practice to find several of his patients suffering the paranoid delusion that their friends or relatives are impostors. He is initially skeptical, especially when the alleged doppelgangers are able to answer detailed questions about their victim's lives, but he is eventually persuaded that something odd has happened and determines to find out what is causing this phenomenon.

item.title: Windmill Productions: A Trio of Poe - A Reader's Theatre
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Edgar Allan Poe is called an American literary genius and here are three of his short stories dramatized for reader’s theater. “The Masque of the Red Death" is a fantastic tale of how, after half the people of his country have died, Prince Prospero gathers knights and ladies to his castle and locks the doors to avoid the devastating “red death” disease. The Trio also includes "The Cask of Amontillado" and "Lionizing" where Poe mocks authors who get recognition when they write nothing but trivia and nonsense in flowery and literary styles. It is true satire and works well between two works of macabre horror.

item.title: B2wins
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Fun-loving twin brothers present "Top 40 From the Last 40": four decades of the music you know and love with a unique twist. This unforgettable, engaging show is for all ages and features one-of-a-kind renditions of everything from Sinatra to Ed Sheeran, Louis Armstrong to Dua Lipa, Michael Jackson to Daft Punk. Get ready to sing, dance, laugh and take your soul on a feel-good ride through the chart-topping hits of American music.

Our stores will be beautifully decorated, spreading holiday cheer at every corner.

To make your shopping experience even more delightful, we're offering shopping passports with exclusive discounts to downtown retail locations. Kick off the shopping season with amazing deals and find the perfect gifts for your loved ones.

Don't miss out on this wonderful opportunity to explore our charming downtown and get into the holiday spirit. Mark your calendars and join us for a weekend filled with joy, laughter, and fantastic shopping.

More details on specials and additional weekend events to come soon.

This fabulous show celebrates everything Bobby Darin – teen idol, singer-songwriter, movie star and nightclub performer: his music, his life and times. Snap your fingers to great Darin hits like “Mack The Knife,” “Splish Splash,” “Dream Lover,” “Beyond the Sea,” “Clementine,” and many more.

Generously sponsored by Mick and Jane Reed

item.title: Brewfontaine Oktoberfest Week
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Prost!!!! Oktoberfest is here!!

We will be celebrating Oct 2nd through Oct. 6th at Brewfontaine with German-inspired food specials all week and lots of different Oktoberfest beers to try! Various glassware giveaways throughout the week.

Monday Special:
Gourmet German Style Smash Burger: 2 smash patties, Oktoberfest beer cheese, caramelized onions, and dijon mustard on a Bavarian Pretzel bun.

Food Specials starting Tuesday:
-Beer Brat and Kraut Soup: Bratwurst, sauerkraut, onion, celery, garlic, cheddar cheese, and smoked Gouda, in a dark stout and beef base topped with shredded cheddar cheese.

-Oktoberfest Salad Trio: German potato salad (warm), bratwurst pasta salad (cold), Eiersalot egg salad (cold) Served with crackers on a bed of lettuce.

-Traditional Beer Brats: 2 Beer brats topped with kraut, raw red onion, sweet relish, ketup and mustard.

-Pumpkin Cheesecake Cup

Thursday at 6:00 will be our first annual Steinholding competition. The two strongest competitors will each win a $100.00 Brewfontaine gift card.

Only 24 spots available for this competition, be sure to secure your spot here: https://www.eventbrite.com/.../hi-wire-oktoberfest...

Immediately following The Steinholding Competition stay for a Trivia Tap Takeover with Hi-Wire Brewing
Prizes include glassware, shirts, and gift cards. Trivia will cover sports, beer, music, and more.
No need to come with a team (or do), you can sign up when you get here to have a good time.

item.title: Murder Mystery Dinner: I Love The 80's To Death
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Join us for a 4-course Murder Mystery Dinner. The last one sold out fast. It’s the 80s again, and all of the big names are here for a big concert. Tensions run rampant as backstage secrets spill out into the spotlight with like, totally tragic results! This isn’t an episode of Miami Vice! This is like…murder. It’s up to you to bust it like a ghost, for sure! Peg your jeans, slouch your socks, flash some neon, put on way too much eyeliner and get that hair up! You are going to solve this crime in style. No time to be Too Shy! You are a Smooth Operator, and you know to listen to those Careless Whispers. It’s time to put on your Sunglasses at Night and Relax. Time after time…You will solve the case.

Grab your friends and come enjoy this unique event! https://resy.com/.../i-love-the-80-s-to-death-2023-10-25

1st Course

Bacon Wrapped BBQ Prawns –
Smokey Bourbon Sauce, Roasted Butternut Squash Salsa
or
Savory Pumpkin Hummus -
Chickpea & Roasted Pumpkin Puree, Olive Oil, Garlic, Sage, Feta Crumbles, Warm Curried Pita Chips

2nd Course

Fall Panzanella Salad –
Warm Crusty Bread, Mixed Greens, Pomegranate, Baby Beets, Candied Pecans, Pickled Red Onion, Buffalo Mozzarella, Honey Truffle Vinaigrette

3rd Course

10 oz. Grilled Pork Mignon –
Dark Cherry Marsala Sauce, Roasted Root Vegetables, Smashed Redskin Potatoes
or
Rosemary & Lemon Half Roasted Chicken –
Roasted Root Vegetables, Smashed redskin Potatoes, Roasted Chicken Jus

4th Course

Candy Pumpkin & Spice Crème Brulé –
Baby Pumpkins, Pumpkin & Spice Custard, Raw Sugar Crust, Whipped Chantilly cream

Reservations: https://resy.com/.../i-love-the-80-s-to-death-2023-10-25

item.title: Zer0 K Fun Run Brought To You By Roundhouse Depot/The Craft Emporium/Brewfontaine
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Brought To You By Derive Brewing, Roundhouse Depot, Craft Emporium & Brewfontaine

Secure your spot here:
https://app.moonclerk.com/pay/9hx1nndv9ql

10/7
10:00am-Whenever You Want

Have you ever wanted to run a 5K but you’re too damn lazy and they never have beer? Well, we have just the event for you!

Join us for the First Annual Zero K Fun Run in Downtown Bellefontaine. BTown’s craft beer dream team made the trip to Derive Brewing in Clintonville and brewed up a very special beer for this event.

Zer0 K Fun Run is a Double Dry Hopped Hazy IPA, featuring Citra Idaho 7, and Citra cryo. Super juicy with tons of ripe pineapple and blast of orange. Very balanced with a light bitterness from the Idaho 7.

Entry fee for this Zero K Run is $35 and includes a draft pint the collab at Brewfontaine, a 16oz can at Craft Emporium and a draft pint at Roundhouse Depot, as well as an exclusive Zer0 K Fun Run tee shirt. Lots of free swag will be handed out along the way.

Competitors can start at whatever location they want and go at their own pace. Sweat bands, leg warmers and shortie shorts are encouraged and a prize will be awarded for our favorite outfit. Special release tappings and can releases will be scheduled throughout the day at each location. Grab a DORA cup and make the trip back and forth as many times as you want!

More info on releases TBD. Can art coming soon!

Pick up your registration packet at Craft Emporium any time during business hours on 10/5 or 10/6. Registration packet will include your event tee, 16oz Can of Zer0 K Fun Run Double Dry Hopped Hazy IPA, punch card passport for race drinks, as well as other goodies!

Secure your spot here:
https://app.moonclerk.com/pay/9hx1nndv9ql

item.title: Macramé Plant Hanger Workshop at Eden Haus
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
November is the perfect time to get together with friends and family! Join us for a fun, hands-on, Macrame Plant Hanger Workshop! Two new, local businesses are teaming up to bring this workshop to Bellefontaine.

This will be a beginner friendly, macramé plant hanger workshop. Your instructor Jess will guide you through some of the basic macramé knots and how to put them together to make something beautiful. In such a fast-paced world, macramé is a wonderful way to slow down and truly be present in your craft.

The Details:
Mark your calendars for Sunday, November 5th from 2-4 PM. This event will be held at Eden Haus in Bellefontaine, Ohio. All materials will be provided.

Tickets:
$35 per guest and you get to take home your finished plant hanger. No refunds for no shows. In the event you must cancel your reservation, if its 72-hour notice, you may request a refund via Eventbrite or email triplemoonproducts@gmail.com and be refunded. No-shows and cancellations made the same day as class will not be refunded. You must be registered for the event to take the workshop; no same day registrations or walk-ins. No children under the age of 9 please.

The Venue:
Our workshop will be indoors at Eden Haus at 118 W Chillicothe Ave in Bellefontaine.

item.title: Brunch With Bob Lucas
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: We are blessed to have the musical talent of Bob Lucas! Bob is a glory shouting, sweet singing, banjo picking, guitar thumping, old time fiddling, song writing rounder with a desire to share a sense of musical wonder anyway he can.

item.title: Charcuterie Class and Thanksgiving Wine Paring for Two
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: We are fortunate to collaborate with Amanda from Cork + Board. She is renowned for her expertise in wine knowledge and for creating amazing charcuterie boards and snacks. She will teach you how to create your own Charcuterie Board for the holidays. Each ticket includes one charcuterie board to build and share or take home, along with complimentary wine flights (up to two people) for Holiday pairing education. We have added 2 classes for you, but don't wait she is so sought after we expect both classes to sell out.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Charcuterie Board and Wine Tasting Tickets - 5:30 pm

item.title: Charcuterie Class and Thanksgiving Wine Paring for Two
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: We are fortunate to collaborate with Amanda from Cork + Board. She is renowned for her expertise in wine knowledge and for creating amazing charcuterie boards and snacks. She will teach you how to create your own Charcuterie Board for the holidays. Each ticket includes one charcuterie board to build and share or take home, along with complimentary wine flights (up to two people) for Holiday pairing education. We have added 2 classes for you, but don't wait she is so sought after we expect both classes to sell out.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Charcuterie Board and Wine paring tickets - 7:30 PM

item.title: Day of the Dead Party
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us for Humberto's tamales, speciality cocktails, prizes for best painted faces and Day of the Dead attire. Live music, and great memories of our dearly departed!

item.title: Kylie Frey with Brandi Sparks
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Fresh from her appearance this season of America’s Got Talent, Kylie has had seven number ones on the country music charts. She’s a third-generation rodeo girl and Louisiana state goat-tying champion – and a rising star as genuine as her blue jeans. Sincere in her convictions and too real to dismiss, even for ardent country haters, her sound both typifies and transcends the genre. Joining her on bass is local musician Jesse Jervis.

Generously sponsored by Marker, Inc.

Season Sponsors: Beasley Architecture & Design and Marker, Inc.; Media Sponsors: Bellefontaine Examiner and V-Teck Communications; Season Support: Honda of America and Ohio Arts Council

item.title: Paint & Plant
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Amy’s Plant Haus & Herb’n Stones want you to paint a Gnome planter then help you fill it with soil and We will have “Easy Plants” to choose from the day of!
$40 a ticket! We are having two classes back to back, kid friendly as well! Get your tickets today!

item.title: LoCo Depot Thanksgiving Day WOD
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us for a workout before all of your Thanksgiving festivities. We are offering 2 options for this workout - 6 AM for those that would like to take on the full workout or 7 AM for those that want a modified version that will be designed for no longer than an hour. Either way, we would love to have you on November 23rd.

item.title: Sofa Express Grand Opening Celebration
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Let’s get the party started on West Columbus Avenue! Join us for the GRAND OPENING of the future of the furniture industry: Sofa Express!

Furniture shopping has never been easier, more affordable, or more FUN. Join us Saturday, November 4th from 10-4 and let us show you a good time. We’ve got an open bar from our friends at Craft Emporium, snacks catered by The Syndicate, and we’re spinning tunes all day. The first 12 families through the door will get a swag bag and we’re giving away a $500 shopping spree to one lucky winner!

Not in the market for new furniture? No worries! Simply make a social media post about our store while you’re in and we’ll give you a free Native gift card. But if you ARE in the market, we’ve got the absolute best prices in the industry plus special deals valid one day only.

Come see what all the fuss is about in Downtown Bellefontaine! Tag a friend and meet us there.

item.title: Titanic
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Celebrate with someone you love at this FREE movie! Eighty-four years later, a 100-year-old woman named Rose DeWitt Bukater tells the story to her granddaughter Lizzy Calvert, Brock Lovett, Lewis Bodine, Bobby Buell and Anatoly Mikailavich on the Keldysh about her life in April of 1912, on a ship called Titanic. This classic tearjerker celebrates the power of love to transcend barriers.

Free admission | Ticket required

item.title: November Plant Swap
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Bring a plant, cutting, or nothing at all! You can take some and/or leave some. I narrowed down the time in hopes of everyone being able to stick around and chat about their plant. If you don’t have time.. don’t worry. Drop you plant off with a care card!

item.title: Homeschool Kids Free Christmas Craft Party
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Join us for a free, kid’s holiday craft event! We’re welcoming our homeschool friends to create a perler bead holiday ornament. Get creative and hang with friends!

The Details: Join us Friday, November 17th at Triple Moon Creative Studio in downtown Bellefontaine. We will be making perler bead holiday ornaments, coloring coloring pages, and hanging with friends! Because of the nature of the craft we are making this one 6 years and up (those perler beads are small!). I will have coloring pages for any younger siblings. All materials will be provided. We request parents stay onsite unless other arrangements are made. Feel free to grab a coffee and relax in the shop!

Tickets: Tickets are free! Registration is only required so we can prepare the craft ahead of time!

Venue: Triple Moon is a new studio space located in the Mainstreet Marketplace (Previously known as the Center City Mall) in downtown Bellefontaine. 130 S. Main St. Unit 104, Bellefontaine, OH 43311. There is free parking on Main Street in the front of the building and also in the parking lot in the back of the building.

item.title: Christmas Cookie Class
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Get ready to decorate and indulge in the sweetest holiday tradition at our Christmas Cookie Class - a hands-on, in-person event!
Christmas Cookie Class

Join us for a fun and festive Christmas Cookie Class at Triple Moon Design Co.! Get into the holiday spirit and learn how to create delicious and beautiful cookies that will impress your friends and family.

During this in-person event, our expert instructor Emily from Rosie Cheeks Bakery will guide you through the process of decorating a variety of Christmas-themed cookies. From classic gingerbread men to adorable candy designs, you'll learn all the tips and tricks to make your cookies look as good as they taste.

Whether you're a beginner or an experienced baker, this class is suitable for everyone. We provide all the ingredients and equipment, so all you need to bring is your enthusiasm and a love for cookies!

Don't miss out on this opportunity to learn new skills and have a jolly good time. Sign up now for the Christmas Cookie Class at Triple Moon Design Co. and let's spread some holiday cheer!

The Tickets:
- $35 per guest and you get to take home your finished cookie set.
- No refunds for no shows. In the event you must cancel your reservation, if its 72-hour notice, you may request a refund via Eventbrite or email triplemoonproducts@gmail.com and be refunded.
- If someone in your party does not come, you may not take home their materials.
- No-shows and cancellations made the same day as class will not be refunded.
- You must be registered for the event to take the workshop; no same day registrations or walk-ins.

The Venue:
Our studio is located in the Main Street Marketplace (previously known as the Center City Mall) in downtown Bellefontaine, Ohio. When you arrive at the Marketplace there is free parking in the front and back of the building and you may enter from both sides.

Triple Moon
130 S. Main St. Unit 104, Bellefontaine, OH 43411

item.title: JingLe jAm! - Kids Day Out
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
We're having our first ever Jingle Jam, Kid's Day Out event at The Photo Booth! Take advantage of small business Saturday in downtown Bellefontaine by stopping at your favorite coffee shop, grabbing lunch, and getting all your Christmas shopping done while your kiddos are having a blast at the studio. Dress them up in their Christmas jammies and we'll take some fun Christmas photos, decorate a Christmas cookie, craft and wrap a special gift all while listening to Christmas tunes. We'll even have a favorite Christmas movie playing! You can choose from three time slots: 9-11am, 11:30 - 1:30pm, or 2:00-4:00pm. A maximum of 20 kids per session, up to 12 yrs old, and potty trained. We will have plenty of elves to entertain so your kiddos are in great hands!

Cost is $45/child and includes a Christmas photo, cookie, craft, snacks and drinks, and lots of Christmas magic!!

item.title: Kegs-N-Eggs OSU vs Michigan
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Join us for our annual Kegs-N Eggs
Osu v/s Michigan game.
Flying Pepper will be serving up their famous Breakfast Burritos and more.

item.title: Drinksgiving
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us for a fun night of catching up with friends visiting for the Holidays. Enjoy a beer with friends.

item.title: Thanksgiving Eve Bash
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us on the eve of Turkey Day for a special Wednesday dinner service followed by Kaitlyn Schmit & Friends with live tunes from 9-midnight! No cover charge, doors open at 8:30 PM. Want to have dinner before the show? Make your reservation here: https://resy.com/.../thanksgiving-eve-dinner-service-2023...

item.title: Brunch with Santa
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us for brunch and photos with the big man! This event is free, just make a reservation. Our full brunch menu will be available as well as Santa crafts and holiday drinks for you and the kiddos! Make your reservation here: https://resy.com/.../events/brunch-with-santa-2023-12-03

item.title: Holiday Dueling Pianos
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Dueling Pianos performs your favorite holiday songs, bring your requests! Tickets are $25 and cover admission only. A full dinner and bar menu will be available on the night of the event. Tickets are extremely limited for this event! Doors open at 5:30 PM. Make your reservation here: https://resy.com/.../holiday-dueling-pianos-2023-12-13

item.title: Brewfontaine Birthday Bash and Thanksgiving Eve Celebration
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Brewfontaine is turning 8 years old! Come celebrate 2,920 days of Brew Crew madness!

1:00pm
Raise your glasses because Mayor Stahler will be in the house to officially proclaim November 22nd as “Brewfontaine Day”.

5:00pm
Tapping of Brewfontaine Birthday Cake Stout!!

Our homies over at Dankhouse cooked us up a big 10% Birthday Cake Stout.
This imperial Pastry Stout is loaded with enough dessert goodness you give you a cavity! We are blowing out the candles at 5:00pm!!

6:00-9:00
Brewfontaine Trivia

Come test your knowledge of 8 years of Brewfontaine shenanigans. Winning teams will take home gift cards, march and more!!

9:00-Midnight
After trivia be sure to finish your drinks and head next door to The Syndicate to rock out with Kaitlyn Schmit & Friends!

item.title: Thanksgiving at Brewfontaine
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
This Thanksgiving Brewfontaine’s own McKinley Weiss has graciously volunteered to open up the bar for a few hours to give you all a little break from your Thanksgiving prep!

She will be serving up cold beer and cocktails from 10:00-2:00. The kitchen will be closed, but feel free to bring your own snacks.

We will be offering up $14 Growler fills and playing the Thanksgiving cult classic, "Alice's Restaurant Massacree" at noon sharp!

item.title: Max Razinger Live
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Local favorite Max Razinger, from the brother/sister duo SaMax, is coming to Brewfontaine for a special solo performance. 
Come grab a pint and take in covers from a wide range of genres. 
Bring your ponchos, cuz you might even get Purple Rained on.

item.title: Yuletide 5
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Jazz up your holidays with this festive Christmas show featuring some of central Ohio’s most talented jazz musicians. Yuletide 5 members include Devin Coons (saxophone), Myles Bowers (trumpet), Isaac Bednarki (percussion), Dean Altstaetter (piano), and Dean Hulett (bass). This sensational jazz combo brings a unique and festive twist to holiday classics, infusing them with the soulful sounds of the season.

Tickets are $10 adults/$5 youth. On sale to members Nov. 14 and to the public Nov. 16.

item.title: LCSO Community Christmas Movie Day
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Come join LCSO staff for a Movie Day!
Santa will be there after the movie.

item.title: Moon Glow over Main Street: A Solar Eclipse Event
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
You will not want to miss the Downtown Bellefontaine Partnership’s once-in-a-lifetime event for the total solar eclipse! Moon Glow over Main Street - the ultimate Total Solar Eclipse extravaganza, happening April 6-8th! 
Prepare to witness the awe-inspiring spectacle of a Total Solar Eclipse, Downtown Bellefontaine is located right in the line of totality.

Tickets: https://www.eventbrite.com/.../moon-glow-over-main-street...

 All Weekend Long: find delicious treats from food trucks, sip on refreshing beverages in the beer garden, and explore the vibrant atmosphere of Downtown Bellefontaine with DORA. Enjoy live music and electrifying beats from our DJ, take advantage of exclusive discounts with coupon books for downtown shops, and engage young minds with STEM alley activities and kids' passports. Plus, gear up for friendly competitions, explore the merchandise tent, and discover endless entertainment!
 Event Schedule:
Saturday:
• All day: Groove to the tunes of our live DJ while soaking in the festival atmosphere.
• Join the cornhole tournament and showcase your skills!
• Don't miss out on the chance to grab coupon books for fantastic deals at downtown businesses.
• 2 pm: Dive into the delicious madness of the Moon Pie Eating Contest.
• 4 pm: Get your hips moving in the Hula Hoop Contest.

Sunday:
• Enjoy more DJ vibes and live music from 12-2 pm.
• From 2-3 pm, engage in fun games and challenges that will keep the excitement going!
• From 3-5 pm, hear more live music!
• 5-6pm Enjoy a family-friendly competition
• 6 pm- Cap off the weekend with a glow party at The Syndicate.
Monday:
• Start the day off with our DJ at 10 am.
• Followed by more live music from 11-12:30 pm
• Friendly family competition begins at 12:30 pm
• Followed by another artist with live music from 1-2:30
• Witness the Total Solar Eclipse beginning around 2:30 pm in Downtown Bellefontaine
 With live music, games, challenges, and entertainment scattered throughout both days, there's never a dull moment at the Moon Glow over Main Street.
 Don't forget to refuel with delicious offerings from food trucks, quench your thirst with refreshing drinks, and indulge in some retail therapy at our downtown shops - open all weekend long!
 Don't miss out on this once-in-a-lifetime event that promises to be a celestial celebration like no other. Join us for the Moon Glow over Main Street and make memories that will last a lifetime!

item.title: Christmas Celebration on Ice
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
NEW DATE! Kick off the holiday season with fun for the entire family – an ice spectacular at the Holland! For the first time ever, and with a little Christmas magic, our stage will be transformed into ice and a dazzling winter wonderland! Experience world-class ice-skating, high-flying tricks, special effects, a modern twist on some classic Christmas stories, and all your favorite Christmas carols mixed with brand-new songs – sure to become family favorites!

Generously sponsored by Beasley Architecture & Design

Season Sponsors: Beasley Architecture & Design and Marker, Inc.; Media Sponsors: Bellefontaine Examiner and V-Teck Communications; Season Support: Honda of America and Ohio Arts Council

item.title: WCOC Concert Band Holiday Concert
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Come enjoy the sounds of the season at this FREE concert! Now in its 31st season, the West Central Ohio Community Concert Band is under the direction of Myles Bowers. The all-volunteer band includes amateur musicians, professional musicians, educators, and students hailing from multiple Ohio counties. Tickets for this popular annual event are available on a first-come, first-served basis.

Admission Free | Ticket Required

item.title: An Evening with Darin & Brooke Aldridge
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Armed with the talents of the proverbial Triple Threat of uniquely distinctive singing, commanding instrumental skills, and accomplished songwriting, Darin and Brooke continue to ascend to new heights in the industry while maintaining their easy-going, down-to-earth connection with audiences everywhere. This husband and wife duo have performed at the Grand Ole Opry more than 50 times.

item.title: Dallas String Quartet | Strings Unleased
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
A brand-new rock fusion experience and electric evening of favorite rock songs perfect for a date night or the entire family! This one-of-a-kind show is brimming with your favorite classic rock songs – from Led Zeppelin, Metallica, AC/DC, Journey, Bon Jovi, Guns N’ Roses and more – played on electric string instruments. Your ears will be delighted and your feet will be tapping!

Generously sponsored by Bellefontaine Examiner

Season Sponsors: Beasley Architecture & Design and Marker, Inc.; Media Sponsors: Bellefontaine Examiner and V-Teck Communications; Season Support: Honda of America and Ohio Arts Council

item.title: Couples Therapy
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: A show unlike any other (we know you’ve heard that before but we mean it)! This unique and clever combination of theater and stand-up comedy is as funny as it is insightful. Audiences go on a hilarious and interactive ride as a pair of married relationship experts equally explore and appreciate the differences between men and women. So strap in, as we don’t just break the fourth wall, we smash right through it!

item.title: Return to Light: A Winter Solstice Event
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Winter solstice is known as the shortest and darkest day of the year. Solstice means "sun standing still", and the winter solstice is often celebrated for the gradual return of light. This class will cultivate a beginning for a new stage in life while another stage comes to an end, much like how winter transitions into spring. Allow this class to help you fine tune your intentions on your personal path of growth. Join Addy for an evening of relaxation and intention setting as we celebrate the solstice. This class combines restorative yoga, chanting, reiki, and Thai yoga massage with hot stones. Addy will guide you through the restorative practice and offer hot stone techniques. Midway through the workshop we will break for tea and journaling. A beautiful flower mandala, oils, and crystals will be utilized to enhance your experience. Mark your calendar and join us for an incredible evening. Space is limited.

Book through the MindBody App or www.homegrownyoga.fit.
Personal Investment: $60

item.title: Full Moon Women's Circle with Addy
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Join Addy for an evening of sisterhood, connection, and reflection. Each circle with start and end with hearing every women's voice. During our time together you will connect with other women, journal, and meditate. You’ll leave feeling supported, guided, and held.

Personal Investment: $30
Book through the MindBody App or www.homegrownyoga.fit

item.title: Ladies Night Out
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Feel like your home needs a refresh this holiday season? Get the girls together and join us for a special after hours shopping event! We’re staying open late just for you and serving delicious wine and snacks  Sip and shop our vintage Bellefontaine wall art collection, save 15% on all lamps, tables, rugs and accessories, and all accent chairs are buy one get one half off! One night only at Tanger’s Furniture, your source for quality home furnishings for over 77 years

item.title: Restorative Aerial Yoga with Thai Massage + Hot Stones
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Join Homegrown Yoga for a blissful restorative aerial yoga experience, centered around self-care and self-love. In an intimate class limited to just 7 participants, each student will receive a silk eye pillow and hot tea for a truly rejuvenating session.

But what exactly is restorative aerial yoga? The hammocks, suspended only inches from the ground, enable a wide range of gentle movements. This serene practice, complemented by the use of hot stones, melts away tension and enhances circulation and metabolism. It can also promote better sleep and overall well-being.

During each pose, our skilled instructors will place hot stones along your energy lines and provide Thai yoga massage assists to deepen your relaxation in every posture.

About the Instructor:
Meet Addy, who boasts a diverse background in aerial yoga training. Her expertise lies in making aerial yoga more accessible, and she recently completed specialized training in restorative aerial yoga, incorporating hot stones and Thai massage.

Personal Investment: $50
Book your session through the MindBody App or visit www.homegrownyoga.fit for a truly enriching experience.

item.title: Kids Art + Yoga
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
This class is designed for children aged 3-6 and the accompanying adult! Join us for a 75-minute journey of yoga and art exploration. The initial part of the class involves a yoga and mindfulness session, perfect for both big and little participants to enjoy together. Strengthen your connection through movement!

After the yoga session, students will create an art project to take home as a keepsake.

Personal Investment: $30
Book your spot through the MindBody App or at www.homegrownyoga.fit

item.title: Holiday Kids Aerial Yoga Camp
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Need a few uninterrupted hours of last minute holiday prep?! We've got you covered at Homegrown!! Campers will enjoy 2 hours of aerial yoga and crafting while you can attend to the many extra responsibilities that come along with the season. Each completed craft item can also be given as a gift! We'll provide all supplies; including the yoga mat! Suitable for ages 6-11.

Sessions dates and times:
December 21st 4:30-6:30 pm
December 22nd 1:30-3:30 pm
December 23rd 11:30 am-1:30 pm

Single Session: $50
3 Sessions: $125

item.title: Christmas Mini Sessions with Indigo and Lace Photography
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Schedule your family Christmas portraits! $125, 5 images. Sign up here: https://book.usesession.com/s/cuExkR4wj

item.title: Santa Mini Sessions with Indigo and Lace Photography
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Enjoy a Christmas Mini Session your family will remember for decades! Indigo & Lace Photography presents the 2023 Santa mini session! This is a festive and magical event that captures the joy and excitement of the holiday season! During this special session, families have the opportunity to create lasting memories by bringing their children to meet and interact with Santa Claus in a cozy and beautifully decorated setting.

Two packages to choose from!!

item.title: Ugly Sweater Party
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us for our 1st Ugly Sweater Party. Enjoy the spirit of Christmas Cheer. Prizes for ugliest and best couple. Flying Pepper will be serving delicious eats 5-8.

item.title: Holiday Paint & Sip - Hot Cocoa Edition
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Spend quality time together this holiday season with a creative paint night! Get inspired as you create a hot cocoa masterpiece on canvas. We'll provide a delicious cocoa bar to fuel your creativity while you work! Combine your favorite holiday treats with a unique experience gift. Paint and sip, you won't want to miss this!

The Details:
Mark your calendars for Friday, December 15th from 6:00-8:30PM. This event will be held at Triple Moon Creative Studio and Gift Shop in Bellefontaine, OH. We will be using acrylic paints on canvas. Expect a hot cocoa bar, snacks, and feel free to BYOB . All materials will be provided and you get to take home your finished painting.

Tickets:
- $25 per guest and you get to take home your finished painting.
- No refunds for no shows. In the event you must cancel your reservation, if its 72-hour notice, you may request a refund via Eventbrite or email triplemoonproducts@gmail.com and be refunded.
- No-shows and cancellations made the same day as class will not be refunded.
- If someone in your party does not come, you may not take home their materials.
- You must be registered for the event to take the workshop.
- No children under the age of 9 please.

The Venue:

Our studio is located in the Main Street Marketplace in downtown Bellefontaine, Ohio. When you arrive at the Marketplace there is free parking in the from and back of the building and you may enter from both sides.

130 S. Main St. Unit 104
Bellefontaine, OH 43311

item.title: Brewfontaine Presents: Carols for a Cause 2023
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Join us for a night of great music from John Whitehead & Friends!
There will be tons of guest appearances from an all star line up of your favorite musicians, as well as stellar draft line up from R. Shea Brewing.

10% of the sales for the day will be donated to Peak of Hope Shelter. (Dine-in and carry-out)

Peak of HopeShelter is run by a board of volunteers, and their mission is to empower the residents of Logan County to achieve shelter stability and self-sufficiency by offering a hopeful hand-up to those in crisis without discrimination. The organization has two homes located in Bellefontaine, that give people a place to stay as they navigate to their forever homes.

We will also be collecting supplies for the shelter. Preferred supplies are things such as:
Blankets
Towels
Washcloths
Twin size bedding
Toothbrushes
Toothpaste
Deodorant
Nail clippers
Body wash
Soap
10-gallon totes for storage

We will be announcing the lineup of confirmed musicians as well at the R. Shea draft selections soon!

item.title: Moonlit Mushroom Paint Night
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Let your inner artist soar with a cozy moonlit mushroom painting night! You'll follow along with an experienced artist and learn some acrylic painting techniques, as you capture the enchanting mushroom landscape of the night. Invite a small group of friends and get ready to candlelight, converse, and create something beautiful. An unforgettable evening awaits!

The Details:
Mark your calendars for Friday, January 5th from 6:00-8:30PM. This event will be held at Triple Moon Creative Studio and Gift Shop in Bellefontaine, OH. Expect snacks and feel free to BYOB . All materials will be provided and you get to take home your finished painting.

Tickets:
- $25 per guest and you get to take home your finished painting.
- No refunds for no shows. In the event you must cancel your reservation, if its 72-hour notice, you may request a refund via Eventbrite or email triplemoonproducts@gmail.com and be refunded.
- No-shows and cancellations made the same day as class will not be refunded.
- If someone in your party does not come, you may not take home their materials.
- You must be registered for the event to take the workshop.
- No children under the age of 9 please.

The Venue:

Our studio is located in the Main Street Marketplace in downtown Bellefontaine, Ohio. When you arrive at the Marketplace there is free parking in the from and back of the building and you may enter from both sides.

130 S. Main St. Unit 104
Bellefontaine, OH 43311

item.title: Ken Chaffin Live
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Kenny is a Marysville, Ohio native who has recently begun his journey as a singer/songwriter. In 2022 he took the stage for the first time, covering songs ranging from all of your 90’s country favorites to current Morgan Wallen hits. He is quickly gaining traction through TikTok and Instagram after being featured on the Columbus radio station, 92.3 WCOL. Kenny has played in the Columbus area building a local fan base. In 2022, Kenny opened for Ernest and also opened for Lainey Wilson at the 2023 Rt. 4 Farm Tour in Marysville, OH. Additionally, he opened for The Wallflowers at the All Ohio Balloon Rally and has also played live in the air for the 92.3 WCOL morning wake up show for Zuko and Kayla.

item.title: Cookie Decorating and Christmas Baking Minis
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Come for an hour filled with Christmas cookie decorating and photos! Learn some cookie decorating skills from City Sweets and Creamery and get a baking mini session with The Photo Booth! Students will decorate 6 cookies to take home, and receive all the digital images from their session and cookie class candids. Event will be held at The Photo Booth, 137 South Main Street!

Choose from from a 4:30 or a 6pm class. Click here to reserve your spot!

https://photoboothbooking.as.me/cookieclass

item.title: House Plant Propagation Workshop
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Come join us at Eden Haus to learn all about how to propagate your favorite houseplants! We will teach you all the basics on how to grow your own houseplant collection from your existing collection. This class will use basic water propagation and soil propagation methods. However, for those interested, and time dependent, air-layering may be discussed. Participants will have the opportunity to take home cuttings from the class which may range from Philodendrons, Tradescantia's, Monstera's, Syngonium's, Snake plants, ZZ plants, or whatever other variety of plants may be available from the greenhouse. There is no guarantee a certain plant will be available as they will be ordered the week prior to the class. Space will be limited. Snacks and beverages will be provided.

item.title: Corey Harris & Cedric Watson / True Blues
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: True Blues chronicles the extraordinary living culture of the blues with acclaimed roots musicians Corey Harris and Cedric Watson. Guitarist, vocalist, songwriter, bandleader, and MacArthur fellow Harris has carved out his own niche in the blues tradition. He has performed, recorded, and toured with many of the top names in music such as B.B. King, Taj Mahal, Buddy Guy, R.L.Burnside, The Dave Matthews Band, Tracy Chapman, and Wilco, among others, and was a featured artist and narrator of the Martin Scorcese 2003 film “Feel Like Going Home,” which traced the evolution of blues from West Africa to the southern U.S. Harris shares the stage with one of the brightest young talents to emerge in Cajun, creole and zydeco (Louisiana French) music over the last decade, Cedric Watson—a four-time Grammy-nominated fiddler, singer, accordionist, and songwriter with seemingly unlimited potential.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Corey Harris & Cedric Watson / True Blues

item.title: Staghorn Fern Mounting Workshop
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded: Join us for a hands-on workshop, you will mount a 4" Staghorn Fern to your choice of live edge wood. Mounting materials will include Staghorn Fern sphagnum moss, twine, and nails. We will have our staff available to assist and provide direction on how to properly mount your Staghorn. There will also be an information session on how to properly care for you Staghorn once it is mounted. Visit www.edenhausplants.com to purchase tickets. Space is limited to 10 participants. If there is need to add another time slot we can do so

item.title: Windmill Productions: Moana Jr.
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Local middle school students present this joyous adaptation of Disney’s beloved classic. This heartwarming coming-of-age story follows the strong-willed Moana as she sets sail across the Pacific to save her village. Moana and the legendary demigod Maui embark on an epic journey of self-discovery and camaraderie as both learn to harness the power that lies within. With empowering messages of bravery and selflessness, Moana JR. is sure to bring out the hero within each of us.

Performances at 7:30 p.m. Friday, Feb. 16; and 2 p.m. and 7:30 p.m. Saturday, Feb. 17

item.title: The Great Dubois
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
A fast-paced, high energy circus show for all ages showcasing incredible feats of juggling, hula hoops, unicycle, aerial, circus stunts, contortion, magic and audience interaction all wrapped up in a blanket of comedy. Simply put: the most thrilling two-person circus show you will ever see!
Featured in Hugh Jackman’s hit movie “The Greatest Showman,” The Great DuBois have amassed an impressive list of credits, including the Tony Award winning “Pippin” on Broadway, “The Tonight Show with Jay Leno,” “Late Night with David Letterman,” Britney Spears World Circus Tour, and the movie “Burlesque.”

Generously sponsored by V-Teck Communications

Season Sponsors: Beasley Architecture & Design and Marker, Inc.; Media Sponsors: Bellefontaine Examiner and V-Teck Communications; Season Support: Honda of America and Ohio Arts Council

item.title: GNO Downtown Bellefontaine's Galentines Night Out
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Galentine's Night Out 

 Date: Tuesday, February 13th
 Time: 5:00 PM - 9:00 PM
 Location: Downtown Bellefontaine & The Flying Pepper Cantina

Join us for an unforgettable GNO~Galentine's Night Out in downtown Bellefontaine! Gather your girl gang and celebrate friendship with an evening filled with shopping, sipping, mingling, and fabulous prizes. Here's what we have planned for you:

5:00 PM - 7:00 PM: Downtown Shop Hop
Embark on a delightful shopping spree with your besties and explore fun experiences offered by participating downtown businesses. It's the perfect opportunity to support local and discover unique finds!

7:00 PM - 9:00 PM: Ladies-Only After Party
After shopping to your heart's content, head over to the Flying Pepper Cantina for an exclusive ladies-only after party. Mingle with your new besties, enjoy specialty cocktails available for purchase, and let the good times roll!

 Special Features 

- Passport Fun - Grab a passport at the beginning of your adventure. Complete it by visiting participating businesses for a chance to win fabulous prizes!
- Prizes Galore - Look forward to winning prizes for the Cutest Dressed Galentine's Group, completing your passport, and more! Plus, snag some door prize tickets at the after party for extra chances to win. Prizes thanks to the generous support of the Downtown Bellefontaine Partnership

 Dress Code: Come dressed in your Galentine's best! Whether it's sparkles, pink, or whatever makes you feel fabulous, let's make it a night to remember.

Stay tuned for more details on participating businesses, specialty cocktails, and prize announcements. Mark your calendars, invite your friends, and get ready for a Galentine's Night Out filled with fun, friendship, and unforgettable memories.

See you there, lovelies!

item.title: 6th Annual Girl Scout Cookie & Beer Pairing
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Join us for our Girl Scout Cookie & Beer Pairing on Tuesday, February 27th from 6:00 - 7:30 PM, one of our most popular events of the year!

Each ticket includes five different 5oz beers paired with five different Girl Scout cookie varieties (2 cookies per variety). Including a never-before-released cookie! In addition to the cookies and beer, ticket holders will get half off of appetizers and $5 pints!! Full boxes of cookies can be purchased at the event as well!

This event is general admission seating on the back patio at Brewfontaine. If you would like to be sat with someone in particular, or a certain group, come 10-15 early to have your best shot at preferred seating.

Pairing #1
Toast Yay!
YAY! French Toast-inspired cookies dipped in delicious icing and full of flavor in every bite!
Paired with Eagle Brewery Banana Bread Beer
Fruit Beer 5.2%
A silky, crisp, and rich amber-colored ale with a fluffy head and a strong banana note on the nose.

Pairing #2
Caramel & Chocolate Chip
Savor the fresh baked taste of Caramel and Chocolate Chip chips with this tasty treat.
Paired with Yellow Springs Moon Milk
Milk Stout 5.5%
This slightly sweet Stout has a chocolate aroma and flavor. The addition of lactose gives it a creamy mouth feel and a smooth finish makes this a super drinkable milk stout.

Pairing#3
Thin Mints
Crisp, chocolate cookies dipped in a delicious mint chocolaty coating.
Paired with Troegs Grand Cacao
Chocolate Milk Stout 6.5%
Cold-steeping on Peruvian cacao nibs and natural vanilla doubles down on the smooth symphony of chocolate, and a splash of milk sugar delivers a velvety sweet and creamy finish.

Pairing #4
Samoas
Crisp cookies with caramel, coconut, and chocolaty stripes .
Paired with Sonder Coconut Tears
English Brown Ale 5.8%
Superbly soft, with toasted coconut, vanilla, and cocoa. A silky texture leads to a long and velvety conclusion.

Pairing #5
Do-si-dos
Crunchy oatmeal sandwich cookies with peanut butter filling.
Paired with Blakes Triple Jam
Cider 6.5%
Inspired by the juicy berries we grow on our farm each summer, Triple Jam is the perfect balance of ripe sweetness with bold fruit aromatics. Bursting with strawberries, blackberries, and raspberries, this semi-sweet captivates the nuance of jam, in each and every taste.

item.title: Eric Nassau Live
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Raised on Connecticut Coffeehouses, fine-tuned in the Midwestern Cornfields. Troubadour with a memory like a steel trap. If Eric has not played your town before - it is only a matter of time...
"One last thing. Who is Eric Nassau? Is best friend you ain't seen in a long time, is rock-salt and whiskey-dipped licorice. Is an Iowa radio tower 2:35 a.m. and buzzing honey goldenrod field high noon. Saw-toothed and wide-eyed. Voice, strings, words. You and me."

item.title: Bellefontaine's Brews & Blarney Crawl
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Join Us for Downtown Bellefontaine’s 1st Annual Brews & Blarney Crawl

Get ready to don your green, grab your friends, and embark on a spirited adventure through downtown Bellefontaine! Our St. Patrick’s Day celebration is not just a beer crawl; it's a treasure trove of fun, laughter, and cheers.

**Date:** Saturday, March 16
**Time:** 6 PM - 12 AM

We will start the shenanigans at 6 PM, where you'll be treated to an array of local brews, each offering a taste of Bellefontaine's finest. As you weave through the heart of downtown, expect more than just a pint at every turn. Engage in lively activities, challenges, and games designed to delight and surprise you at every corner.

End with a Bang at The Syndicate! The frolic doesn't end as the crawl concludes. Join us at The Syndicate for the official after-party starting at 9 PM. The night will come alive with green spirits, live music, dancing and malarkey, making it the perfect climax to an extraordinary evening.

More to Come! This is just a taste of what we have in store. Expect more details on exclusive brews, special guests, and additional activities to be announced. Keep an eye out for updates and surprises that will make Bellefontaine’s Brews & Blarney Crawl a night to remember.

Tickets & Information! Stay tuned for ticket release and further information on how you can secure your spot on this unforgettable night in Downtown Bellefontaine.
